# Handoff: Architect → Supervisor

## Task IDs

| Role | Task ID | Title |
|------|---------|-------|
| REQUEST | bestiary-64s | Implement dayvidpham/bestiary Go module + CLI |
| URD | bestiary-0pi | User Requirements Document |
| RATIFIED PROPOSAL | bestiary-tgf | PROPOSAL-2 (ratified) |
| ELICIT | bestiary-bl1 | URE survey results |
| RESEARCH | bestiary-7iw | models.dev API analysis |
| UAT | bestiary-aat | Plan UAT results |

## Context

bestiary is a Go module (github.com/dayvidpham/bestiary) + CLI that wraps the
https://models.dev/api.json API. It provides:
1. Importable Go bindings — strongly-typed providers, model IDs, static model registry
2. HTTP client — fetches and parses models.dev API with retry
3. SQLite cache — persists synced models for offline list/show
4. CLI — bestiary sync, bestiary list, bestiary show with JSON/YAML/table output
5. Codegen — go:generate tool fetches API and writes static model data

Downstream consumer: dayvidpham/provenance will go get github.com/dayvidpham/bestiary
and call bestiary.StaticModels() to replace hardcoded ml_models seed SQL.

## Key Decisions

1. Provider: type Provider string with 4 well-known constants (Anthropic, Google, OpenAI, Local). IsKnown() method. Any provider slug from API is valid.
2. ModelInfo: All 17 API fields. LastSynced field for merge timestamp. Cost fields as *float64, others as zero-value types.
3. Modality: type Modality int enum with 5 constants (Text, Image, PDF, Audio, Video). Bounds-checked String().
4. Static data: Codegen from API (~110 models from 3 providers). go:generate go run ./cmd/bestiary-gen.
5. SQLite cache: zombiezen.com/go/sqlite (CGO-free). DB at XDG_CACHE_HOME/bestiary/models.db, --db-path override.
6. Merge: MergeModels(static, cached) — deduplicate by ModelID, most-recent LastSynced wins.
7. CLI behavior: list/show=offline (static + cache), sync=online (fetches and persists).
8. YAML: Internal minimal serializer (~50 lines, write-only). No external YAML dep.
9. Client: Functional options pattern. 10 MB response limit. 2 retries with backoff.
10. Errors: Struct types ErrNotFound, ErrAPIUnavailable (use errors.As, not errors.Is).

## Open Items

None — all decisions resolved through URE + UAT.

## Implementation Slices (Suggested by Architect)

### Slice 1: Core Types (Layer 0)
- bestiary.go, provider.go, modality.go, errors.go
- go.mod with zombiezen.com/go/sqlite

### Slice 2: Wire Types + Client
- wire.go, client.go, client_test.go
- 10 MB limit, retry, context cancellation

### Slice 3: SQLite Store
- store.go, store_test.go
- Schema, UpsertModels, QueryModels, DefaultDBPath

### Slice 4: Codegen + Static Registry
- cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go, models_static_gen.go
- registry.go, registry_test.go

### Slice 5: Merge + Format + CLI
- merge.go, merge_test.go, format.go, format_test.go
- cmd/bestiary/main.go with --db-path

## Acceptance Criteria

See PROPOSAL-2 (bestiary-tgf) design field for full validation checklist (22 items)
and acceptance criteria (7 BDD scenarios).

## Source Material

Extract and adapt from ~/dev/agent-data-leverage/develop/:
- internal/store/models_data.go — static model data pattern
- internal/store/models_client.go — HTTP client with retry
- internal/ingest/store_iface.go:138-154 — original ModelInfo struct

## Constraints

- Go 1.24+, CGO_ENABLED=0
- Dependencies: stdlib + zombiezen.com/go/sqlite only
- Use git agent-commit for commits
- Model IDs use hyphens (matching models.dev): claude-opus-4-6, NOT claude_opus_4

## Planning Phases Complete

- Phase 1: REQUEST captured and classified (bestiary-64s)
- Phase 2: URE completed (bestiary-bl1), URD created (bestiary-0pi)
- Phase 3: PROPOSAL-1 (bestiary-j5r, superseded) then PROPOSAL-2 (bestiary-tgf)
- Phase 4: 3 reviewers ACCEPT PROPOSAL-2 (bestiary-xny, bestiary-93h, bestiary-j35)
- Phase 5: UAT-1 REVISED (bestiary-aat) — added SQLite cache layer
- Phase 6: PROPOSAL-2 ratified
- Phase 7: This handoff"}

## Specification

Foundation layer: Go module init, all core types, and their methods. Every other slice depends on this.

## Files Owned
- go.mod (module github.com/dayvidpham/bestiary, go 1.24, require zombiezen.com/go/sqlite)
- go.sum (generated)
- bestiary.go (package doc, ModelID, ModelInfo)
- provider.go (Provider type, constants, IsKnown, String, MarshalText, UnmarshalText)
- modality.go (Modality enum, Modalities struct, String with bounds check, MarshalText, UnmarshalText)
- errors.go (ErrNotFound, ErrAPIUnavailable with Error() methods)

## Type Contracts (from PROPOSAL-2)

### ModelID (bestiary.go)
type ModelID string

### ModelInfo (bestiary.go)
type ModelInfo struct {
    ID                    ModelID
    Provider              Provider
    DisplayName           string
    Family                string
    ContextWindow         int
    MaxOutput             int
    Reasoning             bool
    ToolCall              bool
    Attachment            bool
    Temperature           bool
    StructuredOutput      bool
    Interleaved           bool
    OpenWeights           bool
    CostInputPerMTok      *float64
    CostOutputPerMTok     *float64
    CostReasoningPerMTok  *float64
    CostCacheReadPerMTok  *float64
    CostCacheWritePerMTok *float64
    ReleaseDate           string
    Knowledge             string
    Modalities            Modalities
    LastSynced            string   // RFC3339
}

### Provider (provider.go)
type Provider string
const (
    ProviderAnthropic Provider = "anthropic"
    ProviderGoogle    Provider = "google"
    ProviderOpenAI    Provider = "openai"
    ProviderLocal     Provider = "local"
)
func (p Provider) IsKnown() bool — returns true for the 4 constants
func (p Provider) String() string
func (p Provider) MarshalText() ([]byte, error)
func (p *Provider) UnmarshalText(b []byte) error

### Modality (modality.go)
type Modality int
const (
    ModalityText  Modality = iota
    ModalityImage
    ModalityPDF
    ModalityAudio
    ModalityVideo
)
func (m Modality) String() string — MUST bounds-check: if m < 0 || int(m) >= len(modalityNames) return fmt.Sprintf("Modality(%d)", m)
type Modalities struct { Input []Modality; Output []Modality }
MarshalText/UnmarshalText for Modality

### Errors (errors.go)
type ErrNotFound struct { What, Key string } — func (e *ErrNotFound) Error() string
type ErrAPIUnavailable struct { URL string; Attempts int; Cause error } — func (e *ErrAPIUnavailable) Error() string; func (e *ErrAPIUnavailable) Unwrap() error
Use errors.As (not errors.Is) for these structured types.

## Source Material
Adapt from ~/dev/agent-data-leverage/develop/internal/ingest/store_iface.go:138-154 for ModelInfo struct.
Helper functions pattern: ~/dev/agent-data-leverage/develop/internal/store/models_data.go (strPtr, intPtr, floatPtr)

## Acceptance Criteria
Given bestiary package imported When accessing Provider constants Then ProviderAnthropic == "anthropic", etc.
Given Modality(99) When calling String() Then returns "Modality(99)" not panic
Given ErrNotFound{What: "model", Key: "foo"} When using errors.As Then matches *ErrNotFound

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] go.mod declares module github.com/dayvidpham/bestiary with go 1.24
- [ ] All 17 ModelInfo fields match proposal exactly
- [ ] Provider.IsKnown() true for 4 constants, false for arbitrary strings
- [ ] Modality.String() bounds-checks (no panic on out-of-range)
- [ ] MarshalText/UnmarshalText round-trip for Provider and Modality
- [ ] Error types implement error interface with actionable messages
- [ ] ErrAPIUnavailable implements Unwrap() for errors.As chain

{"id":"bestiary-bye","title":"SLICE-2: Wire Types + HTTP Client","description":"---
references:
  impl_plan: bestiary-pe7
  urd: bestiary-0pi
  proposal: bestiary-tgf
  depends_on_slice: bestiary-nry
---
## Specification

HTTP client for models.dev API with functional options, retry, and 10 MB response limit. Wire types for JSON deserialization from API.

## Files Owned
- wire.go (wire types matching models.dev JSON, toModelInfo converter)
- client.go (Client struct, functional options, FetchModels, FetchModelsByProvider)
- client_test.go (mock httptest.Server tests)

## Type Contracts

### Wire Types (wire.go)
Internal types matching models.dev/api.json structure:
- wireResponse = map[string]wireProvider
- wireProvider struct { Models map[string]wireModel }
- wireModel with all 17 fields from API (JSON tags matching API)
- wireCost struct with 5 *float64 fields
- wireLimit struct with Context, Output *int
- toModelInfo(providerSlug string, wm wireModel) ModelInfo — converts wire → domain
- toModalities(input, output []string) Modalities — converts string slices to typed Modalities

### Client (client.go)
type Client struct { httpClient *http.Client; baseURL string; retries int; timeout time.Duration }
type ClientOption func(*Client)
func WithTimeout(d time.Duration) ClientOption
func WithRetries(n int) ClientOption
func WithBaseURL(url string) ClientOption
func NewClient(opts ...ClientOption) *Client — defaults: 30s timeout, 2 retries, "https://models.dev/api.json"
func (c *Client) FetchModels(ctx context.Context) ([]ModelInfo, error)
func (c *Client) FetchModelsByProvider(ctx context.Context, p Provider) ([]ModelInfo, error)

### Key Implementation Details
- Response capped at 10 MB via io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 10*1024*1024)
- Retry: exponential backoff time.Duration(attempt) * time.Second, respects ctx.Done()
- On final failure: return ErrAPIUnavailable{URL, Attempts, lastErr}
- FetchModelsByProvider: fetches all, filters by provider (API has no per-provider endpoint)
- Set LastSynced = "" on fetched models (caller sets timestamp on persist)

### Integration Point: IP-2
- Owner: This slice (Slice 2)
- Consumer: Slice 4 (codegen uses wire types for API parsing)
- Contract: wire.go exports wireResponse type and toModelInfo function
- NOTE: wire types are unexported (lowercase) but in same package, so codegen cmd in cmd/bestiary-gen/ will need to use the Client.FetchModels() public API instead

## Source Material
Adapt from ~/dev/agent-data-leverage/develop/internal/store/models_client.go:
- Retry pattern (lines 73-91)
- 10 MB limit pattern (line ~140)
- Wire type structure (lines 33-69)

## Acceptance Criteria
Given mock server returning valid JSON When FetchModels called Then returns []ModelInfo with correct field mapping
Given mock server returning 500 When FetchModels called with 2 retries Then returns ErrAPIUnavailable after 3 attempts
Given context cancelled during retry When waiting for backoff Then returns ctx.Err() promptly
Given response > 10 MB When reading body Then truncated by LimitReader, returns error

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] Client uses io.LimitReader with 10 MB cap
- [ ] Client retries with exponential backoff (mock test verifies attempt count)
- [ ] Client respects context cancellation
- [ ] Wire types correctly parse models.dev JSON structure
- [ ] toModelInfo maps all 17 fields correctly
- [ ] FetchModelsByProvider filters correctly
- [ ] ErrAPIUnavailable returned on exhausted retries (use errors.As)
- [ ] go test -race ./... passes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-04T13:11:02Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-04T13:11:02Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-cka","title":"SLICE-3: SQLite Store","description":"---
references:
  impl_plan: bestiary-pe7
  urd: bestiary-0pi
  proposal: bestiary-tgf
  depends_on_slice: bestiary-nry
---
## Specification

SQLite-backed cache for synced model data. Uses zombiezen.com/go/sqlite (CGO-free). Stores models fetched via sync command for offline list/show access.

## Files Owned
- store.go (Store struct, schema, Open, Close, UpsertModels, QueryModels, QueryModel, DefaultDBPath)
- store_test.go (in-memory SQLite tests)

## Type Contracts

### Store (store.go)
type Store struct { conn *sqlite.Conn; path string }

func DefaultDBPath() (string, error) — returns $XDG_CACHE_HOME/bestiary/models.db, falls back to ~/.cache/bestiary/models.db

func OpenStore(path string) (*Store, error) — opens/creates SQLite DB, creates models table if not exists

func (s *Store) Close() error — closes the database connection

func (s *Store) UpsertModels(ctx context.Context, models []ModelInfo) error — INSERT OR REPLACE, sets LastSynced to current UTC RFC3339

func (s *Store) QueryModels(ctx context.Context, provider Provider) ([]ModelInfo, error) — returns all cached models; if provider is empty ("") returns ALL models; if provider specified, filters by provider

func (s *Store) QueryModel(ctx context.Context, id ModelID) (ModelInfo, error) — returns single model by ID; returns ErrNotFound if not found

### SQL Schema
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS models (
    model_id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    provider          TEXT NOT NULL,
    display_name      TEXT NOT NULL,
    family            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    context_window    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    max_output        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    reasoning         INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    tool_call         INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    attachment        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    temperature       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    structured_output INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    interleaved       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    open_weights      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    cost_input        REAL,
    cost_output       REAL,
    cost_reasoning    REAL,
    cost_cache_read   REAL,
    cost_cache_write  REAL,
    release_date      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    knowledge         TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    modalities_input  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    modalities_output TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    last_synced       TEXT NOT NULL
);

### Key Implementation Details
- Use sqlitex.ExecuteTransient for schema creation (one-off)
- Use sqlitex.Execute for queries (cached prepared statements)
- Booleans stored as INTEGER 0/1
- *float64 fields: NULL when nil, REAL when non-nil
- Modalities: comma-separated string ("text,image")
- QueryModels with empty provider means "return all" (no WHERE clause)
- UpsertModels sets LastSynced = time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
- Use sqlitex.Transaction for bulk upsert

### zombiezen.com/go/sqlite API Reference
- sqlite.OpenConn(path) — open connection
- sqlitex.ExecuteTransient(conn, sql, nil) — one-off DDL
- sqlitex.Execute(conn, sql, &sqlitex.ExecOptions{Args: []any{...}, ResultFunc: func(stmt *sqlite.Stmt) error {...}})
- sqlitex.Transaction(conn) — returns func(*error) for deferred commit/rollback
- stmt.GetText("col_name"), stmt.GetInt64("col_name"), stmt.GetFloat("col_name"), stmt.GetBool("col_name"), stmt.IsNull("col_name")

### Integration Point: IP-3
- Owner: This slice (Slice 3)
- Consumer: Slice 5 (CLI sync/list/show commands use Store)
- Contract: OpenStore, UpsertModels, QueryModels, QueryModel, Close

## Source Material
Adapt from ~/dev/agent-data-leverage/develop/internal/store/models.go:
- SQL upsert pattern, null handling helpers (boolToInt, derefFloat64)
- Column scanning pattern (scanModelInfo)

## Acceptance Criteria
Given in-memory SQLite When UpsertModels with 3 models Then QueryModels returns 3 models with correct fields
Given models upserted When QueryModels with provider="anthropic" Then returns only Anthropic models
Given models upserted When QueryModels with provider="" Then returns ALL models
Given no model with ID "foo" When QueryModel("foo") Then returns ErrNotFound (use errors.As)

{"id":"bestiary-cka","title":"SLICE-3: SQLite Store","description":"---
references:
  impl_plan: bestiary-pe7
  urd: bestiary-0pi
  proposal: bestiary-tgf
  depends_on_slice: bestiary-nry
---
## Specification

SQLite-backed cache for synced model data. Uses zombiezen.com/go/sqlite (CGO-free). Stores models fetched via sync command for offline list/show access.

## Files Owned
- store.go (Store struct, schema, Open, Close, UpsertModels, QueryModels, QueryModel, DefaultDBPath)
- store_test.go (in-memory SQLite tests)

## Type Contracts

### Store (store.go)
type Store struct { conn *sqlite.Conn; path string }

func DefaultDBPath() (string, error) — returns $XDG_CACHE_HOME/bestiary/models.db, falls back to ~/.cache/bestiary/models.db

func OpenStore(path string) (*Store, error) — opens/creates SQLite DB, creates models table if not exists

func (s *Store) Close() error — closes the database connection

func (s *Store) UpsertModels(ctx context.Context, models []ModelInfo) error — INSERT OR REPLACE, sets LastSynced to current UTC RFC3339

func (s *Store) QueryModels(ctx context.Context, provider Provider) ([]ModelInfo, error) — returns all cached models; if provider is empty ("") returns ALL models; if provider specified, filters by provider

func (s *Store) QueryModel(ctx context.Context, id ModelID) (ModelInfo, error) — returns single model by ID; returns ErrNotFound if not found

### SQL Schema
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS models (
    model_id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    provider          TEXT NOT NULL,
    display_name      TEXT NOT NULL,
    family            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    context_window    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    max_output        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    reasoning         INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    tool_call         INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    attachment        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    temperature       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    structured_output INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    interleaved       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    open_weights      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    cost_input        REAL,
    cost_output       REAL,
    cost_reasoning    REAL,
    cost_cache_read   REAL,
    cost_cache_write  REAL,
    release_date      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    knowledge         TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    modalities_input  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    modalities_output TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    last_synced       TEXT NOT NULL
);

### Key Implementation Details
- Use sqlitex.ExecuteTransient for schema creation (one-off)
- Use sqlitex.Execute for queries (cached prepared statements)
- Booleans stored as INTEGER 0/1
- *float64 fields: NULL when nil, REAL when non-nil
- Modalities: comma-separated string ("text,image")
- QueryModels with empty provider means "return all" (no WHERE clause)
- UpsertModels sets LastSynced = time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
- Use sqlitex.Transaction for bulk upsert

### zombiezen.com/go/sqlite API Reference
- sqlite.OpenConn(path) — open connection
- sqlitex.ExecuteTransient(conn, sql, nil) — one-off DDL
- sqlitex.Execute(conn, sql, &sqlitex.ExecOptions{Args: []any{...}, ResultFunc: func(stmt *sqlite.Stmt) error {...}})
- sqlitex.Transaction(conn) — returns func(*error) for deferred commit/rollback
- stmt.GetText("col_name"), stmt.GetInt64("col_name"), stmt.GetFloat("col_name"), stmt.GetBool("col_name"), stmt.IsNull("col_name")

### Integration Point: IP-3
- Owner: This slice (Slice 3)
- Consumer: Slice 5 (CLI sync/list/show commands use Store)
- Contract: OpenStore, UpsertModels, QueryModels, QueryModel, Close

## Source Material
Adapt from ~/dev/agent-data-leverage/develop/internal/store/models.go:
- SQL upsert pattern, null handling helpers (boolToInt, derefFloat64)
- Column scanning pattern (scanModelInfo)

## Acceptance Criteria
Given in-memory SQLite When UpsertModels with 3 models Then QueryModels returns 3 models with correct fields
Given models upserted When QueryModels with provider="anthropic" Then returns only Anthropic models
Given models upserted When QueryModels with provider="" Then returns ALL models
Given no model with ID "foo" When QueryModel("foo") Then returns ErrNotFound (use errors.As)
Given UpsertModels called twice with same ID When QueryModel Then returns version with most recent LastSynced

- [ ] *float64 fields round-trip through NULL correctly
- [ ] Modalities serialize/deserialize as comma-separated strings
- [ ] DefaultDBPath uses XDG_CACHE_HOME with fallback

{"id":"bestiary-4r4","title":"SLICE-4: Codegen + Static Registry","description":"---
references:
  impl_plan: bestiary-pe7
  urd: bestiary-0pi
  proposal: bestiary-tgf
  depends_on_slices: bestiary-nry,bestiary-bye
---
## Specification

Code generation tool that fetches models.dev API and writes models_static_gen.go with ~110 static models. Registry functions query the compiled-in static data.

## Files Owned
- cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go (go:generate tool — fetches API, writes models_static_gen.go)
- models_static_gen.go (GENERATED file — static model data for anthropic, google, openai)
- registry.go (StaticModels, LookupModel, ModelsByProvider, ModelsByFamily)
- registry_test.go (registry query tests)

## Type Contracts

### Registry (registry.go)
func StaticModels() []ModelInfo — returns copy of compiled-in models (does NOT set LastSynced; codegen sets it at generation time)
func LookupModel(id ModelID) (ModelInfo, bool) — returns model + found flag
func ModelsByProvider(p Provider) []ModelInfo — filters static models by provider
func ModelsByFamily(family string) []ModelInfo — filters static models by family

### Generated File (models_static_gen.go)
// Code generated by bestiary-gen. DO NOT EDIT.
//go:generate go run ./cmd/bestiary-gen
package bestiary

var staticModels = []ModelInfo{
    {ID: "claude-opus-4-6-20250514", Provider: ProviderAnthropic, ...LastSynced: "2026-04-04T..."},
    // ~110 entries
}

CRITICAL: Codegen MUST set LastSynced on each static model to the generation timestamp (RFC3339 UTC). This is needed for merge precedence (most-recent-wins).

### Codegen Tool (cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go)
- Fetches https://models.dev/api.json
- Parses response using encoding/json (defines own internal wire types OR uses Client)
- Filters to anthropic, google, openai providers only (excludes aggregators)
- Generates Go source: models_static_gen.go with staticModels slice
- Sets LastSynced = generation timestamp on each model
- Uses go/format to produce gofmt-clean output

### Key Implementation Details
- The codegen tool is package main in cmd/bestiary-gen/ (separate binary)
- It cannot import unexported wire types from the bestiary package
- It should define its own minimal JSON parsing types OR use NewClient().FetchModels()
- Using NewClient().FetchModels() is cleaner — reuses existing client
- Registry functions use the package-level staticModels var (populated by generated code)
- StaticModels() returns a defensive copy (append([]ModelInfo(nil), staticModels...))
- Model IDs use hyphens matching models.dev (claude-opus-4-6, NOT claude_opus_4)

### Integration Point: IP-4
- Owner: This slice (Slice 4)
- Consumer: Slice 5 (CLI list/show uses StaticModels, LookupModel, etc.)
- Contract: registry.go exports StaticModels, LookupModel, ModelsByProvider, ModelsByFamily

## Source Material
Adapt from ~/dev/agent-data-leverage/develop/internal/store/models_data.go:
- Static model data pattern (38 models → ~110 models)
- StaticModels() function pattern (copy + timestamp)
Note: bestiary codegen SETS timestamp at generation time, not at runtime like source material.

## Acceptance Criteria
Given go generate ./... runs When API is reachable Then models_static_gen.go written with ~110 models from 3 providers
Given generated file When StaticModels() called Then returns ~110 entries with LastSynced set
Given generated file When LookupModel("claude-opus-4-6-20250514") Then returns (model, true)
Given generated file When ModelsByProvider(ProviderAnthropic) Then returns only Anthropic models
Given generated file When ModelsByProvider(Provider("openrouter")) Then returns empty slice

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] go generate ./... produces valid models_static_gen.go
- [ ] Generated file passes go vet
- [ ] StaticModels returns ~110 entries from 3 providers (anthropic, google, openai)
- [ ] Each static model has LastSynced set to generation timestamp
- [ ] LookupModel returns (info, true) for known IDs
- [ ] ModelsByProvider filters correctly
- [ ] ModelsByFamily filters correctly
- [ ] No aggregator providers included in generated data
- [ ] StaticModels returns a defensive copy (modifying result doesn't affect registry)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-04T13:11:50Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-04T13:11:50Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-h8h","title":"SLICE-5: Merge + Format + CLI","description":"---
references:
  impl_plan: bestiary-pe7
  urd: bestiary-0pi
  proposal: bestiary-tgf
  depends_on_slices: bestiary-nry,bestiary-bye,bestiary-cka,bestiary-unk
---
## Specification

Merge logic (static + cached dedup), output formatting (JSON/YAML/table), and CLI entry point wiring everything together.

## Files Owned
- merge.go (MergeModels function)
- merge_test.go
- format.go (FormatModels, FormatModel — JSON, YAML, table output)
- format_test.go
- cmd/bestiary/main.go (CLI entry point)

## Type Contracts

### Merge (merge.go)
func MergeModels(static, cached []ModelInfo) []ModelInfo
- Deduplicates by ModelID
- When both sources have same ModelID, most-recent LastSynced wins (RFC3339 string comparison is lexicographic, valid for UTC timestamps)
- Returns merged, deduplicated list
- Edge cases: empty static, empty cached, both empty, timestamp ties (either wins)

### Format (format.go)
type OutputFormat string
const (
    FormatJSON  OutputFormat = "json"
    FormatYAML  OutputFormat = "yaml"
    FormatTable OutputFormat = "table"
)

func FormatModels(w io.Writer, models []ModelInfo, format OutputFormat) error — writes formatted list
func FormatModel(w io.Writer, model ModelInfo, format OutputFormat) error — writes formatted single model

JSON: encoding/json with MarshalIndent
YAML: Internal minimal serializer (~50 lines, write-only, no external dep)
Table: Human-readable tabular format (ID, Provider, Family, Context, Cost columns)

### CLI (cmd/bestiary/main.go)
Usage:
  bestiary list [--provider <slug>] [--format json|yaml|table] [--db-path <path>]
  bestiary show <model-id> [--format json|yaml|table] [--db-path <path>]
  bestiary sync [--provider <slug>] [--format json|yaml] [--db-path <path>]

Behavior:
  list: StaticModels() + store.QueryModels() → MergeModels → FormatModels (offline)
  show: LookupModel() then store.QueryModel() → pick most-recent → FormatModel (offline)
  sync: NewClient().FetchModels() → store.UpsertModels() → FormatModels (online)

--db-path defaults to DefaultDBPath()
--format defaults to "json"
--provider filters list/sync by provider

### Key Implementation Details
- CLI uses flag package (no external CLI framework)
- YAML serializer: ~50 lines, handles flat struct fields, *float64 as null/number, []Modality as list
- Table format: fixed-width columns, header row, aligned output
- Error handling: os.Exit(1) with message to stderr
- If store cannot be opened (e.g., first run), list/show fallback to static-only

### Integration Points Consumed
- IP-1: ModelInfo, Provider, Modality from Slice 1
- IP-2: Client.FetchModels from Slice 2
- IP-3: Store.OpenStore, UpsertModels, QueryModels, QueryModel from Slice 3
- IP-4: StaticModels, LookupModel, ModelsByProvider from Slice 4

## Acceptance Criteria
Given static=[A:ts1] cached=[A:ts2>ts1] When MergeModels Then returns [A:ts2] (cached wins)
Given static=[A:ts2] cached=[A:ts1<ts2] When MergeModels Then returns [A:ts2] (static wins)
Given models When FormatModels(json) Then valid JSON output
Given models When FormatModels(yaml) Then valid YAML output (no external dep)
Given models When FormatModels(table) Then readable table with headers
Given bestiary list --provider anthropic When static + cache available Then shows merged Anthropic models
Given bestiary sync --provider anthropic When API reachable Then fetches, stores, outputs
Given --db-path /tmp/test.db When running command Then uses that path, not XDG default

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] MergeModels deduplicates by ModelID, most-recent-wins
- [ ] MergeModels handles edge cases (empty inputs, timestamp ties)
- [ ] JSON output validates with encoding/json
- [ ] YAML output is valid (internal serializer, no external dep)
- [ ] Table output is human-readable with aligned columns
- [ ] CLI list command works offline (static + cache merge)
- [ ] CLI sync command fetches and persists
- [ ] CLI show command finds model by ID
- [ ] --db-path flag overrides default
- [ ] --format flag switches output format
- [ ] --provider flag filters results
- [ ] Graceful fallback when store unavailable (list/show use static only)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-04T13:12:13Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-04T13:12:13Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-jbb","title":"FIX-SLICE-1: Capability type for Interleaved field","description":"---
references:
  uat: bestiary-7wx
  impl_plan: bestiary-pe7
---
## Fix Description (BLOCKER)

The interleaved field in models.dev API is polymorphic: `true`/`false` or `{"field": "reasoning_details"}`. The object form means the capability IS supported with additional config. Current flexBool treats objects as false — incorrect.

## Changes Required

### bestiary.go — Add Capability type, change ModelInfo.Interleaved
```go
// Capability represents a model capability that may carry additional configuration.
// When Supported is true and Config is non-nil, the config provides details
// (e.g. {"field": "reasoning_details"} for interleaved).
type Capability struct {
    Supported bool
    Config    map[string]string
}
```
Change `Interleaved bool` → `Interleaved Capability` in ModelInfo.

### wire.go — Replace flexBool with proper Capability handling
- Keep flexBool for other fields (Reasoning, ToolCall, etc. are always bool in API)
- Change wireModel.Interleaved to json.RawMessage
- In toModelInfo: parse Interleaved as bool or object → Capability
- If bool true: Capability{Supported: true}
- If bool false: Capability{Supported: false}
- If object {"field": "reasoning_details"}: Capability{Supported: true, Config: map[string]string{"field": "reasoning_details"}}

### store.go — Schema change for interleaved
Replace single INTEGER column with two columns:
```sql
interleaved       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- stays: Supported bool
interleaved_config TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',    -- new: JSON-encoded Config map
```
Update UpsertModels: serialize Config as JSON string (or "" if nil)
Update scanModelInfo: deserialize Config from JSON string
NOTE: This is a breaking schema change. Use DROP TABLE IF EXISTS + CREATE TABLE (dev only, no prod data to migrate).

### format.go — Update output for Capability
- JSON: Capability struct serializes naturally via json.Marshal
- YAML: show "interleaved: true" or "interleaved: true (field: reasoning_details)"
- Table: show true/false for Interleaved column (ignore config in table)

### cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go — Handle polymorphic interleaved in codegen
Update the codegen wire types to use json.RawMessage for interleaved, same as existing handling.
Generate Capability literals in models_static_gen.go.

### Tests
- Update client_test.go mock to include object interleaved: {"field": "reasoning_details"}
- Update store_test.go to test Capability round-trip
- Update any tests that reference Interleaved as bool
- Regenerate models_static_gen.go

## Files Touched
bestiary.go, wire.go, client.go (no change), client_test.go, store.go, store_test.go, format.go, format_test.go, cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go, models_static_gen.go, registry_test.go","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-04T14:03:50Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-04T14:03:50Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-c8l","title":"FIX-SLICE-2: Composite key (model_id, provider) + table columns","description":"---
references:
  uat: bestiary-7wx
  impl_plan: bestiary-pe7
  depends_on: FIX-SLICE-1
---
## Fix Description (BLOCKER + MINOR)

### Composite key (BLOCKER)
Same model ID can appear under multiple providers with different pricing. Current PRIMARY KEY is model_id only, which collapses cross-provider duplicates.

### Table columns (MINOR)
Add Reasoning and ToolCall boolean columns to table output.

## Changes Required

### store.go — Composite primary key
Change schema: `PRIMARY KEY (model_id, provider)` instead of `model_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY`.
Update QueryModel to accept (ModelID, Provider) or return all provider variants for a model ID.
NOTE: Since FIX-SLICE-1 already changes the schema (adds interleaved_config), this slice builds on that new schema.

### merge.go — Dedup by (ModelID, Provider)
Change map key from ModelID to a struct{ModelID, Provider} tuple or a string key like "model_id|provider".
Dedup logic: when same (ID, Provider) pair exists in both static and cached, most-recent LastSynced wins.

### registry.go — LookupModel behavior
LookupModel currently returns first match by ID. With composite key, same ID may have multiple providers.
Options: (a) LookupModel returns first match (keep simple), (b) add LookupModelByProvider(id, provider).
Recommend: keep LookupModel as-is (returns first match), it's for static registry which only has 3 providers where IDs don't overlap.

### cmd/bestiary/main.go — show command
Show should query by model_id and display all provider variants if multiple exist.
QueryModel should return []ModelInfo (all providers for that ID) or keep single-model but require provider.
Recommend: QueryModel returns first match, add QueryModelsByID(id) that returns all variants.

### format.go — Add Reasoning + ToolCall columns to table
Add two columns after MaxOutput: Reasoning (bool), ToolCall (bool).
Update table header, separator, and row format strings.

### Tests
- Update store_test.go for composite key (same model_id, different providers)
- Update merge_test.go for (ID, Provider) dedup
- Update format_test.go for new table columns

## Files Touched
store.go, store_test.go, merge.go, merge_test.go, registry.go, cmd/bestiary/main.go, format.go, format_test.go","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-04T14:04:05Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-04T14:04:05Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-75q","title":"FIX-SLICE-3: Lightweight schema migrations for SQLite store","description":"---
references:
  uat: bestiary-7wx
  impl_plan: bestiary-pe7
  depends_on: bestiary-c8l
---
## Fix Description (BLOCKER)

Old SQLite databases miss new columns (interleaved_config) and have wrong PRIMARY KEY (model_id only vs composite). Users upgrading bestiary get confusing SQL errors.

## Solution: Lightweight ALTER TABLE migrations

### 1. schema_meta table
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_meta (version INTEGER NOT NULL);
```
If table is empty (fresh DB or pre-migration DB), insert version 0.

### 2. Migration logic in OpenStore
On OpenStore(), after creating schema_meta:
1. Read current version from schema_meta
2. If version < currentSchemaVersion, apply migrations sequentially
3. Update schema_meta.version after each migration

### 3. Schema versions
- Version 0: No schema_meta existed (legacy DB or empty DB)
- Version 1: Original schema (model_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, no interleaved_config)
- Version 2: Current schema (PRIMARY KEY (model_id, provider), interleaved_config column)

### 4. Migration v0/v1 → v2
SQLite doesn't support ALTER TABLE to change PRIMARY KEY. Migration must:
```sql
-- Step 1: Add interleaved_config if missing (safe for v1 DBs)
-- Use pragma to check if column exists, or just try ALTER and ignore error

-- Step 2: Recreate table for composite key
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS models_new (...current schema with PRIMARY KEY (model_id, provider)...);
INSERT OR IGNORE INTO models_new SELECT model_id, provider, ..., COALESCE(interleaved_config, '') FROM models;
DROP TABLE models;
ALTER TABLE models_new RENAME TO models;

-- Step 3: Update version
INSERT OR REPLACE INTO schema_meta (rowid, version) VALUES (1, 2);
```

### 5. Fresh DB path
For a fresh database (no models table exists), just create with current schema and set version=2. No migration needed.

### 6. Constants
```go
const currentSchemaVersion = 2
```

## Files Touched
- store.go (migration logic in OpenStore, schema_meta table, version constants)
- store_test.go (test migration from v1 schema, test fresh DB gets v2)

## Testing
- TestMigration_FreshDB: OpenStore on empty DB → version 2, correct schema
- TestMigration_V1toV2: Create v1 schema manually, insert data, OpenStore → data preserved, composite key works, interleaved_config column exists
- TestMigration_V2Idempotent: OpenStore twice on same DB → no error, version still 2","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-04T14:21:17Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-04T14:21:17Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-gzl","title":"FOLLOWUP_SLICE-3: Code Fixes","description":"---
references:

## Specification
Fix IMPORTANT F5.2,7 and all MINOR findings.

### Files Owned (Production)
- format.go: remove dead writeYAMLModalityList function, extract table const (tableHeader/tableSep dedup)
- client.go: fix docstring "exponential backoff" → "linear backoff"
- merge.go: sort MergeModels output by (Provider, ID) via slices.SortFunc
- store.go: add comment documenting ctx param unused (zombiezen sqlite does not support per-op context)
- cmd/bestiary/main.go: surface QueryModels error in runList (not silently discard), consistent context.Background()
- errors.go: add comment documenting no Unwrap by design (no wrapped cause)

### Files Owned (Tests)
- registry_test.go: TestStaticModels_ThreeProviders (verify exactly 3 known providers)
- store_test.go: TestUpsertModels_EmptySlice (edge case)
- format_test.go: modify existing TestFormatModel_JSON to add structural field check (unmarshal, verify expected keys)

### Requirements

1. format.go fixes:
   - Remove writeYAMLModalityList (dead code — check it is truly unused before removing)
   - Extract tableHeader/tableSep into a shared const or combine format string

2. client.go: change docstring from "exponential backoff" to "linear backoff" on FetchModels

3. merge.go: after building result slice from map, sort with slices.SortFunc by (Provider, ID)
   ```go
   slices.SortFunc(result, func(a, b ModelInfo) int {
       if c := strings.Compare(string(a.Provider), string(b.Provider)); c != 0 {
           return c
       }
       return strings.Compare(string(a.ID), string(b.ID))
   })
   ```

4. store.go: add comment on UpsertModels, QueryModels, QueryModel, QueryModelsByID explaining ctx is accepted for API compatibility but zombiezen/sqlite does not support per-operation context cancellation

5. cmd/bestiary/main.go:
   - In runList: change `cached, _ = store.QueryModels(...)` to handle error (surface to user or log)
   - Ensure context.Background() usage is consistent across runList/runShow/runSync

6. errors.go: add comment on ErrNotFound explaining no Unwrap() because there is no wrapped cause (sentinel-style error)

7. registry_test.go: add TestStaticModels_ThreeProviders — collect unique providers from StaticModels, assert len == 3

8. store_test.go: add TestUpsertModels_EmptySlice — call UpsertModels with empty slice, verify no error and no rows added

9. format_test.go: modify existing TestFormatModel_JSON — unmarshal output to map[string]any, verify expected structural fields present

### Integration Points
- Depends on Slice 1 completing first (go.sum changes from go mod tidy)
- merge.go sort may change test expectations in merge_test.go (existing tests check output content, not order — verify)
- store_test.go also modified by Slice 2 (different test functions)
- format_test.go also modified by Slice 2 (different test functions)

### Acceptance Criteria
Given MergeModels called multiple times with same input
When output compared
Then always returns identical ordering (sorted by Provider, ID)
Should never produce non-deterministic ordering

### Validation Checklist
- [ ] writeYAMLModalityList removed from format.go
- [ ] client.go docstring says "linear backoff"
- [ ] MergeModels output is deterministic (sorted)
- [ ] ctx usage documented in store.go
- [ ] runList surfaces QueryModels errors
- [ ] context.Background() consistent in CLI
- [ ] ErrNotFound documented no Unwrap
- [ ] TestStaticModels_ThreeProviders passes
- [ ] TestUpsertModels_EmptySlice passes
- [ ] TestFormatModel_JSON has structural check
- [ ] go vet ./... passes
- [ ] go test ./... passes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-04T14:59:19Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-04T14:59:19Z"}

references:

## Scope
Add missing test coverage for IMPORTANT findings.

### store_test.go (ADD new test functions)
- TestDefaultDBPath: use t.Setenv("XDG_CACHE_HOME", ...) to test XDG path, then unset for fallback. DefaultDBPath() is in store.go — read it first to understand XDG logic.
- TestUpsertModels_StampsLastSynced: insert models via UpsertModels, then QueryModels and verify LastSynced field is a recent RFC3339 timestamp (within last minute)

### format_test.go (ADD new test functions)
- TestFormatModel_YAML: call FormatModel(w, model, FormatYAML), capture output, validate string contains expected YAML keys: "ID:", "Provider:", "DisplayName:", "Family:"
- TestFormatModels_Table: call FormatModels(w, models, FormatTable), verify output contains table header and model data rows
- YAML validation: string-based key checks only (no yaml.v3 dependency)

### cmd/bestiary/main_test.go (NEW — 4 scenarios)
- TestRun_NoArgs: call run([]string{}) → expect error containing "usage" or similar
- TestRun_UnknownCommand: call run([]string{"bogus"}) → expect error containing "unknown"
- TestRun_List: call run([]string{"list", "--db-path", t.TempDir()+"/test.db"}) → should succeed, verify output
- TestRun_ShowNoID: call run([]string{"show"}) → expect error about missing model ID

Note: CLI tests require understanding run() signature. Read cmd/bestiary/main.go first. The run function likely takes args and returns error. Use t.TempDir() for --db-path to avoid polluting real DB.

## Files Owned
- store_test.go (new test functions only — do not modify existing tests)
- format_test.go (new test functions only — do not modify existing tests)
- cmd/bestiary/main_test.go (NEW)

## Acceptance Criteria
Given DefaultDBPath called with XDG_CACHE_HOME set when testing then returns XDG-based path
Given UpsertModels called when checking LastSynced then timestamp is recent

{"id":"bestiary-gzl","title":"FOLLOWUP_SLICE-3: Code Fixes","description":"---
references:
  impl_plan: bestiary-9r0
  urd: bestiary-4et
  proposal: bestiary-wa2
---
## Specification
Fix IMPORTANT F5.2,7 and all MINOR findings.

### Files Owned (Production)
- format.go: remove dead writeYAMLModalityList function, extract table const (tableHeader/tableSep dedup)
- client.go: fix docstring "exponential backoff" → "linear backoff"
- merge.go: sort MergeModels output by (Provider, ID) via slices.SortFunc
- store.go: add comment documenting ctx param unused (zombiezen sqlite does not support per-op context)
- cmd/bestiary/main.go: surface QueryModels error in runList (not silently discard), consistent context.Background()
- errors.go: add comment documenting no Unwrap by design (no wrapped cause)

### Files Owned (Tests)
- registry_test.go: TestStaticModels_ThreeProviders (verify exactly 3 known providers)
- store_test.go: TestUpsertModels_EmptySlice (edge case)
- format_test.go: modify existing TestFormatModel_JSON to add structural field check (unmarshal, verify expected keys)

### Requirements

1. format.go fixes:
   - Remove writeYAMLModalityList (dead code — check it is truly unused before removing)
   - Extract tableHeader/tableSep into a shared const or combine format string

2. client.go: change docstring from "exponential backoff" to "linear backoff" on FetchModels

3. merge.go: after building result slice from map, sort with slices.SortFunc by (Provider, ID)
   ```go
   slices.SortFunc(result, func(a, b ModelInfo) int {
       if c := strings.Compare(string(a.Provider), string(b.Provider)); c != 0 {
           return c
       }
       return strings.Compare(string(a.ID), string(b.ID))
   })
   ```

4. store.go: add comment on UpsertModels, QueryModels, QueryModel, QueryModelsByID explaining ctx is accepted for API compatibility but zombiezen/sqlite does not support per-operation context cancellation

5. cmd/bestiary/main.go:
   - In runList: change `cached, _ = store.QueryModels(...)` to handle error (surface to user or log)
   - Ensure context.Background() usage is consistent across runList/runShow/runSync

6. errors.go: add comment on ErrNotFound explaining no Unwrap() because there is no wrapped cause (sentinel-style error)

7. registry_test.go: add TestStaticModels_ThreeProviders — collect unique providers from StaticModels, assert len == 3

8. store_test.go: add TestUpsertModels_EmptySlice — call UpsertModels with empty slice, verify no error and no rows added

9. format_test.go: modify existing TestFormatModel_JSON — unmarshal output to map[string]any, verify expected structural fields present

### Integration Points
- Depends on Slice 1 completing first (go.sum changes from go mod tidy)
- merge.go sort may change test expectations in merge_test.go (existing tests check output content, not order — verify)
- store_test.go also modified by Slice 2 (different test functions)
- format_test.go also modified by Slice 2 (different test functions)

### Acceptance Criteria
Given MergeModels called multiple times with same input
When output compared
Then always returns identical ordering (sorted by Provider, ID)
Should never produce non-deterministic ordering

### Validation Checklist
- [ ] writeYAMLModalityList removed from format.go
- [ ] client.go docstring says "linear backoff"
- [ ] MergeModels output is deterministic (sorted)
- [ ] ctx usage documented in store.go
- [ ] runList surfaces QueryModels errors
- [ ] context.Background() consistent in CLI
- [ ] ErrNotFound documented no Unwrap
- [ ] TestStaticModels_ThreeProviders passes
- [ ] TestUpsertModels_EmptySlice passes
- [ ] TestFormatModel_JSON has structural check
- [ ] go vet ./... passes
- [ ] go test ./... passes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"worker-3","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-04T14:59:19Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-04T15:18:31Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-73o","title":"SLICE-3-L3: Impl — production code fixes","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-gzl
  impl_plan: bestiary-9r0
---
## Scope
Fix all IMPORTANT F5.2,7 and MINOR production code issues.

### format.go
- Remove dead writeYAMLModalityList function (verify unused first by grepping)
- Deduplicate tableHeader/tableSep format strings (extract shared const or combine)

### client.go
- Fix docstring on FetchModels: change "exponential backoff" to "linear backoff"
- The actual backoff is time.Duration(attempt) * time.Second — linear, not exponential

### merge.go
- After building result slice from map, sort with slices.SortFunc:
  ```go
  import "slices"
  import "strings"
  slices.SortFunc(result, func(a, b ModelInfo) int {
      if c := strings.Compare(string(a.Provider), string(b.Provider)); c != 0 {
          return c
      }
      return strings.Compare(string(a.ID), string(b.ID))
  })
  ```
- Verify existing merge_test.go still passes (tests check content not order)

### store.go
- Add comment on UpsertModels, QueryModels, QueryModel, QueryModelsByID:
  // ctx is accepted for API compatibility; zombiezen.com/go/sqlite does not support per-operation context cancellation.

### cmd/bestiary/main.go
- In runList: change `cached, _ = store.QueryModels(...)` to handle error:
  ```go
  cached, err = store.QueryModels(ctx, provider)
  if err != nil {
      return fmt.Errorf("query cached models: %w", err)
  }
  ```
  OR log warning and continue with static-only (match runShow pattern)
- Ensure context.Background() usage is consistent across all run* functions

### errors.go
- Add comment on ErrNotFound:
  // ErrNotFound has no Unwrap method by design: it is a sentinel-style error
  // with no wrapped cause. Use errors.As to match.

## Files Owned
- format.go
- client.go
- merge.go
- store.go
- cmd/bestiary/main.go
- errors.go

## Acceptance Criteria
Given MergeModels called multiple times then output order is identical (deterministic)
Given runList encounters QueryModels error then error is surfaced to user
Should never have dead code or misleading docstrings","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-04T15:00:34Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-04T15:00:34Z"}

## Verbatim User Request
"There's a new requirement we should also attend to. It's at issue #2 in this repo."

## Status
This feature was fully ratified in a previous epoch (URE, URD, proposal, review, UAT all completed). The GitHub issue contains the complete spec with exact code. This is ready for direct implementation.

## Ratified Spec (from issue #2)

### harness.go
- type Harness string
- 6 constants: HarnessClaudeCode ("claude-code"), HarnessGeminiCLI ("gemini-cli"), HarnessCodex ("codex"), HarnessOpenCode ("opencode"), HarnessCursor ("cursor"), HarnessAntigravity ("antigravity")
- knownHarnesses array, IsKnown(), String(), MarshalText(), UnmarshalText() — exact same pattern as Provider
- Permissive UnmarshalText (accepts unknown strings, like Provider)

### harness_test.go
- TestHarness_IsKnown: all 6 constants + unknown values (empty, uppercase, unknown strings)
- TestHarness_String: all 6 constants
- TestHarness_MarshalUnmarshalText: round-trip for all 6
- TestHarness_UnmarshalText_Permissive: unknown string accepted, IsKnown() false
- TestHarness_UnmarshalText_NilReceiver: error path

### Acceptance Criteria
1. bestiary.Harness type exists with all 6 constants
2. go test ./... passes
3. API follows identical pattern to bestiary.Provider","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-08T17:09:22Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-08T17:09:22Z"}

{"description":"---
references:
  request: bestiary-u9k
  urd: bestiary-xek
  proposal: bestiary-axj
  handoff: bestiary-iky
---

## Rationale
Generate ~110 Provider constants + family constants from models.dev API, expand static model registry from 3 to 110 providers (~4168 models), scaffold ModelRef type, add configurable codegen filter flags + API response caching, update tests to be property-based, and document all 62 model ID collisions.

## Integration Points

- Consumers: SLICE-2 (bestiary-b62, ModelRef.Provider field), SLICE-3 (bestiary-346, test assertions)
- Contract: Provider type remains `type Provider string`; constants move files but API unchanged
- Merge timing: SLICE-1 must land before SLICE-3 worker starts; SLICE-2 can start in parallel

### IP-2: staticModels expansion (owned by SLICE-1, consumed by SLICE-2 and SLICE-3)
- Owner: SLICE-1 (bestiary-4s6) — regenerates models_static_gen.go from 110 → ~4168 models
- Consumers: SLICE-2 (bestiary-b62, ProvidersForFamily iterates staticModels), SLICE-3 (bestiary-346, property tests)
- Contract: staticModels remains []ModelInfo; only the content grows
- Merge timing: SLICE-2 code compiles against current staticModels; semantics verified after SLICE-1 lands

- Owner: SLICE-1 (bestiary-4s6) — generates knownProviders in providers_gen.go, removes from provider.go
- Consumers: SLICE-3 (bestiary-346, TestProviders_AllKnown, TestProviders_MinimumCount)

{"id":"bestiary-0ip","title":"IMPL_PLAN: Entity normalization pipeline","description":"---
references:
  request: bestiary-n6x
  urd: bestiary-rjf
  elicit: bestiary-73x
  proposal: bestiary-1oq
  handoff: bestiary-28c
  uat: bestiary-qz7
---

## Objective
Decompose the ratified PROPOSAL-3 (bestiary-1oq) into vertical slices with explicit horizontal Layer Integration Points. Each slice owns a full vertical (types → tests → impl → wiring) and is implemented by exactly one worker.

## Decomposition: 8 Vertical Slices

### Slice 1 — parse package + codegen template apply (foundation)
Owner files (NEW): `parse.go`, `parse_test.go`, `parse/data/family_overrides.json`, `parse/data/variant_suffixes.json`, `parse/data/version_patterns.json`.
Touched: `families_gen.go`, `models_static_gen.go`, `providers_gen.go` (regenerate via `go generate ./...` to apply commit 6a8d75b template fix — must end with `type Family string`, NOT `type Family = string`). `.bestiary-gen-cache/api_response.json` (dirty, will be re-cached).

Deliverables:
- `ParseFamily(raw Family) (Family, string)` — uses overrides table → suffix table → versioned-variant patterns (v/k/m/no-prefix). Hyphen-separated versions (claude-opus-4-5) handled. Empty raw families return zero values.
- `ExtractDate(id ModelID, releaseDate string) string` — match YYYYMMDD or YYYY-MM-DD only.
- `inferFamilyFromID(id ModelID, p Provider) Family` — fallback for empty-family models (~25%).
- Embed JSON via `//go:embed parse/data/*.json` (regular package directory, NOT testdata/).
- `go generate ./...` regenerates all `_gen.go` files with named Family type.

### Slice 2 — ModelInfo extension + family methods + codegen normalization splice
Owner files (NEW): `family.go`, `family_test.go`.
Touched: `bestiary.go` (ModelInfo new fields), `cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go` (genToModelInfo at line 524, validateGeneratedFamilyType post-condition).

Deliverables:
- ModelInfo gains `NormalizedFamily Family`, `NormalizedVariant string`, `NormalizedDate string`.
- `family.go`: `Family.IsKnown()`, `String()`, `MarshalText()`, `UnmarshalText()` — resolves debt task bestiary-ptm.
- Codegen splice: invoke `parse.ParseFamily` + `parse.ExtractDate` in `genToModelInfo()` to bake Normalized fields into `models_static_gen.go`.
- `validateGeneratedFamilyType(path)` post-condition runs at end of `run()` and asserts grep for `type Family string` (no `=`).
- TestFamilyType_NamedNotAlias regression test in package root.

### Slice 3 — ModelRef + canonical + designation + Resolve (public API)
Owner files (NEW): `canonical.go`, `designation.go`, `resolve.go`, `canonical_test.go`, `designation_test.go`, `resolve_test.go`.
Touched: `modelref.go` (ModelRef gains ID field, becomes 6-field; Format(scheme) dispatch; Designations() method; Stringer uses SchemeCanonical), `errors.go` (NEW *ErrAmbiguous struct error), `errors_test.go`.

Deliverables:
- `CanonicalScheme` int enum: SchemeCanonical | SchemeHuggingFace | SchemePURL | SchemeRaw (UAT Option A formats).
- `Designation{Value, Scheme, Provider, Rating}`, `AcceptabilityRating` int enum (Admitted | Preferred | Deprecated). All static designations default to Admitted.
- ModelRef 6 fields: ID, Provider, RawFamily, Family, Variant, Date.
- ModelRef.Format(scheme), ModelRef.Designations().
- `Resolve(input string, opts ...ResolveOption) ([]ModelRef, error)` — slice for cross-provider hosting, *ErrAmbiguous for distinct canonicals, ErrNotFound for zero. WithScheme(s) option.

### Slice 4 — Model_ constants generation
Owner files (NEW): `cmd/bestiary-gen` additions for `generateConstantsSource()`, `models_constants_gen.go` (generated), `constants_test.go`.

Deliverables:
- New codegen step: emits `Model_<Provider>_<Family>_<Variant>_<Date>?` constants. Underscore-separated component naming. Provider/Family/Harness keep PascalCase.
- Two-pass collision algorithm: append disambiguator from raw ID version segment, fall back to `_<n>` sequential suffix.
- Skip rule: empty family AND no extractable family from ID → no constant generated.
- `Models() []ModelID` returns all generated constants (defensive copy).
- Tests: TestModelConstants_Unique, TestModelConstants_RoundTrip, TestModelConstants_NamingStable.

### Slice 5 — SQLite v2→v3 migration + QueryByCanonical
Owner files (NEW): `migrations/v2_to_v3.sql`.
Touched: `store.go` (migrateToV3 mirroring migrateToV2 pattern, currentSchemaVersion bump, scanModelInfo + UpsertModels column-list updates, new QueryByCanonical method), `store_migration_test.go`, `store_test.go`.

Deliverables:
- v2→v3 migration: rename existing `family` → `raw_family`; add `family` (parsed), `variant`, `date` columns; create `idx_canonical ON models(family, variant, provider)`.
- Backfill on first migrate: re-run parse logic on existing rows.
- QueryByCanonical(ctx, family, variant, date) ([]ModelInfo, error).
- Standalone SQL file at `migrations/v2_to_v3.sql` containing equivalent ALTER/CREATE statements (reference doc, not executed by Go code).
- Migration test: 5 sub-tests — preserves-data, backfill, idempotent, edge-cases, index-used.

### Slice 6 — CLI extensions (bestiary show + bestiary-gen cache flags)
Touched: `cmd/bestiary/main.go` (show: --scheme=<canonical|huggingface|purl|raw> flag, auto-detect by prefix, ErrAmbiguous candidate-list rendering), `cmd/bestiary/main_test.go`, `cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go` (--cache-dir <path> and --no-fetch flags), `cmd/bestiary-gen/main_test.go`.

Deliverables:
- `bestiary show <input> [--scheme=...]` resolves via Resolve. Auto-detect default. Prints candidate table on *ErrAmbiguous.
- `bestiary-gen --cache-dir <path>` overrides hardcoded `.bestiary-gen-cache/`.
- `bestiary-gen --no-fetch` reads cached api_response.json instead of HTTP. Cache miss → actionable error (path, reason, remediation per C-actionable-errors).

### Slice 7 — Schema + version + MIGRATION docs
Touched: `bestiary.schema.json` (document NormalizedFamily/Variant/Date, ModelRef.ID, Designation array, AcceptabilityRating enum, schema version 0.0.2), `version.go` (BestiarySchemaVersion → "0.0.2", NOT 0.1.0), `schema_test.go`, `version_test.go`. NEW: `MIGRATION_v0.0.1_to_v0.0.2.md`.

Deliverables:
- BestiarySchemaVersion exact string "0.0.2".
- bestiary.schema.json conformance test (TestJSONOutput_ConformsToSchema) passes against new fields.
- MIGRATION doc: ModelInfo new fields, ModelRef.ID, parse package, SQL column renames, Model_ constants naming, schema version bump.

### Slice 8 — Provenance companion PR (separate repo)
Repo: `~/codebases/dayvidpham/provenance/` (NOTE: repo root, not under `/main/`).
Touched: `pkg/ptypes/enums.go` (Provider.IsValid delegates to bestiary.Provider(p).IsKnown(); MarshalText/UnmarshalText permissive), `pkg/ptypes/enums_test.go`, `models_test.go`, `go.mod` (bump bestiary dependency to v0.0.2 — note: current pin is v0.1.0; if release tag differs from BestiarySchemaVersion, surface back to supervisor).

Deliverables:
- Provider remains a wrapper string type but loses 4-value enforcement.
- RegistryFromBestiary unchanged (already a direct cast).
- Tests refactored to drop 4-value assumption; add 110-provider validation case.
- PR ready for coordinated landing once bestiary v0.0.2 is tagged.

## Horizontal Layer Integration Points

| ID | Owner | Consumers | Contract | Merge Timing |
|----|-------|-----------|----------|--------------|
| IP-1 | Slice 1 | All slices | `families_gen.go` declares `type Family string` (named type) | After Slice 1 commits & before Slice 2 starts |
| IP-2 | Slice 2 | Slices 3, 4, 5, 7 | `ModelInfo.NormalizedFamily/Variant/Date` populated by codegen | After Slice 2 commits |
| IP-3 | Slice 3 | Slices 6, 7 | `ModelRef` 6-field shape with `ID ModelID`; `CanonicalScheme` enum; `*ErrAmbiguous` | After Slice 3 commits |
| IP-4 | Slice 1 | Slices 2, 4, 6 | `parse.ParseFamily`, `parse.ExtractDate`, `parse.InferFamilyFromID` public API | After Slice 1 commits |
| IP-5 | Slice 5 | Slice 7 | `family`/`variant`/`date` columns; `idx_canonical` index | After Slice 5 commits |
| IP-6 | Slice 4 | None internal; external Models() | `Model_*` constants + `Models()` API | After Slice 4 commits |
| IP-7 | Slice 7 | Slice 8 | `BestiarySchemaVersion = "0.0.2"` ratified; ready for tag | After Slice 7 commits |

## Build Wave Order

- **Wave 1 (parallel)**: Slice 1, Slice 8 (Slice 8 is independent of bestiary internals; only its go.mod bump waits for v0.0.2 tag).
- **Wave 2 (depends on Slice 1)**: Slice 2.
- **Wave 3 (depends on Slice 2, parallel)**: Slice 3, Slice 4, Slice 5.
- **Wave 4 (depends on Slice 3)**: Slice 6.
- **Wave 5 (depends on Slice 2-5)**: Slice 7.

## Acceptance Criteria (from HANDOFF bestiary-28c)
See full list in handoff. All slices must collectively satisfy:
- `go vet ./...` and `CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./...` pass.
- families_gen.go declares `type Family string` (named type), confirmed by TestFamilyType_NamedNotAlias.
- BestiarySchemaVersion exact string "0.0.2".
- MIGRATION_v0.0.1_to_v0.0.2.md exists.
- migrations/v2_to_v3.sql exists.
- bestiary.schema.json conformance test passes.

## Cross-cutting reminders
- All commits via `git agent-commit -m "..."` (NEVER `git commit`).
- Provider/Family/Harness keep PascalCase (no breaking change). Only Model_* constants use underscore separators.
- Schema 0.0.1 → 0.0.2 (NOT 0.1.0).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T09:49:55Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T09:49:55Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-28c","title":"HANDOFF: Architect → Supervisor for entity normalization","description":"---
references:
  request: bestiary-n6x
  urd: bestiary-rjf
  elicit: bestiary-73x
  proposal: bestiary-1oq (RATIFIED)
  uat: bestiary-qz7
  prior_proposal: bestiary-4m5 (superseded), bestiary-qk1 (superseded)
---

## Handoff: Architect → Supervisor

### Context
Entity normalization pipeline. The deferred work from the codegen epoch (REQUEST bestiary-u9k → URD bestiary-xek) is now in scope. Goal: canonical representation of model and provider names, allowing users to ask "show me Claude Opus 4 anywhere" and find all 4+ provider expressions of the same weights.

### Ratified Plan: PROPOSAL-3 + 5 UAT Revisions

### Deliverables Summary

1. **`parse/data/family_overrides.json`**, **`parse/data/variant_suffixes.json`**, **`parse/data/version_patterns.json`** — embedded JSON data for parsing rules.

2. **`parse.go`** (NEW) — `ParseFamily(raw Family) (Family, string)`, `ExtractDate(id ModelID, releaseDate string) string`, `inferFamilyFromID(id ModelID, p Provider) Family`. NOTE: full rename from "decompose" terminology.

3. **`canonical.go`** (NEW) — `CanonicalScheme` int enum: SchemeCanonical, SchemeHuggingFace, SchemePURL, SchemeRaw. Format definitions per UAT Option A:
   - SchemeCanonical: `<provider>/<family>/<variant>@<date>` (e.g., `anthropic/claude/opus@2025-05-14`) OR a provider's actual representation
   - SchemeHuggingFace: `<provider>/<raw-id>` matching real HF Hub (e.g., `anthropic/claude-opus-4-20250514`)
   - SchemePURL: `pkg:huggingface/<provider>/<raw-id>` (per ECMA-427:2025 + purl-spec PR #201)
   - SchemeRaw: original API ID (e.g., `claude-opus-4-20250514`)

4. **`designation.go`** (NEW) — `Designation{Value, Scheme, Provider, Rating}`, `AcceptabilityRating` int enum (Admitted, Preferred, Deprecated). All static designations default to Admitted.

5. **Updated `modelref.go`** — ModelRef has 6 fields including new `ID ModelID`. Methods: String() (Stringer using SchemeCanonical), Format(scheme), Designations(). Drop Canonical() projector method (Canonical type itself was already dropped in PROPOSAL-2).

6. **Updated `bestiary.go` ModelInfo** — adds `NormalizedFamily Family`, `NormalizedVariant string`, `NormalizedDate string` (codegen-baked).

7. **`resolve.go`** (NEW) — `Resolve(input string, opts ...ResolveOption) ([]ModelRef, error)`. Returns slice for cross-provider hosting (same canonical, different providers). Returns *ErrAmbiguous when input matches multiple distinct canonicals. Returns ErrNotFound for zero matches. WithScheme(s) option pins scheme.

8. **`family.go`** (NEW) — Family.IsKnown(), String(), MarshalText(), UnmarshalText(). Resolves bestiary-ptm follow-up debt. Requires families_gen.go to have `type Family string` (named type), NOT `type Family = string` (alias) — implementation MUST run `go generate ./...` to apply the codegen template fix from commit 6a8d75b.

9. **`models_constants_gen.go`** (NEW, generated) — Per-canonical model ID constants with underscore-separated naming:
   - Format: `Model_<Provider>_<Family>_<Variant>_<Version>?_<Date>?` (only Model constants use underscores; Provider/Family/Harness keep PascalCase — no break)
   - Examples: `Model_Anthropic_Claude_Opus_4_20250514`, `Model_OpenAI_GPT_4o_20240806`, `Model_Google_Gemini_2_5_Flash_Lite_Preview_20250417`
   - Naming derived from parser output components, joined by `_`
   - Within-component characters preserved (e.g., `4o` stays `4o`, not `4_o`)
   - Two-pass collision resolution: append disambiguator from raw ID version segment, fall back to `_<n>` sequential suffix
   - Skip rule: empty family AND no extractable family from ID → no constant generated
   - `Models() []ModelID` returns all generated constants

10. **CLI extension `cmd/bestiary/main.go`** — `bestiary show <input> [--scheme=<canonical|huggingface|purl|raw>]`. Auto-detects scheme by prefix. ErrAmbiguous prints candidate list.

11. **Cache flags `cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go`** — `--cache-dir <path>` and `--no-fetch`. Cache miss with `--no-fetch` → actionable error (path, reason, remediation).

12. **SQLite v2→v3 migration `store.go`** — column renames per UAT:
    - Existing `family` column RENAMED to `raw_family`
    - NEW columns: `family` (parsed), `variant`, `date`
    - NEW index: `idx_canonical ON models(family, variant, provider)`
    - Backfill on first migrate: re-runs parse logic on existing rows
    - NEW Store method `QueryByCanonical(family, variant, date) ([]ModelInfo, error)` exercises the index
    - Plus a separate SQL migration file (testdata/migrations/v2_to_v3.sql or migrations/v2_to_v3.sql)

13. **Provenance companion PR** (separate repo `dayvidpham/provenance`):
    - `provenance.Provider.IsValid()` delegates to `bestiary.Provider(p).IsKnown()`
    - `provenance.Provider.MarshalText()` becomes permissive (any value round-trips)
    - `provenance.Provider.UnmarshalText()` becomes permissive (accepts any string)
    - Adapter `RegistryFromBestiary` retained for ModelInfo conversion
    - Tests updated: remove 4-value assumption, add 110-provider validation
    - go.mod bump to bestiary v0.0.2 once released

14. **`bestiary.schema.json`** — updated to document NormalizedFamily/Variant/Date, ModelRef.ID, Designation array, AcceptabilityRating enum. Schema version `0.0.2`.

15. **Codegen template regeneration** — implementation MUST run `go generate ./...` and verify families_gen.go declares `type Family string` (no `=`). PROPOSAL-3's `validateGeneratedFamilyType` function in codegen guards this post-condition.

16. **Migration documentation**:
    - `MIGRATION_v0.0.1_to_v0.0.2.md` — single document covering: new ModelInfo fields, ModelRef shape change (ID added), parse package introduction, SQL column renames, Model_ constants naming convention, schema version bump
    - SQL migration file: `migrations/v2_to_v3.sql` (or similar location) containing the ALTER TABLE / CREATE INDEX statements

### Key Decisions

1. **No LRM types** — simple canonical triple is the goal (Family, Variant, Date), Provider is metadata.
2. **Parse, not decompose** — terminology rename per UAT.
3. **Suffix table + manual overrides** — deterministic, auditable. Splink overkill at 4,168 records.
4. **Tuple as canonical, multi-format strings** — Format(scheme) dispatch with typed enum (SchemeCanonical default).
5. **All Designations are AcceptabilityAdmitted** — promotion to Preferred deferred to curation epoch.
6. **Bake decomposition into static gen** — runtime Ref() is pure projection. Zero allocation/runtime parsing for the common case.
7. **Embed via decompose/data/ → parse/data/** — JSON files in regular package directory (NOT testdata/), embedded via go:embed for runtime.
8. **Resolve returns []ModelRef + *ErrAmbiguous** — slice for cross-provider hosting (same canonical), error for distinct canonicals.
9. **Provenance: keep type, delegate validation** — provenance.Provider stays; IsValid/Marshal/Unmarshal all become permissive aligned with bestiary's model.
10. **Schema version 0.0.1 → 0.0.2** (NOT 0.1.0) — additive changes, patch bump per user correction.
11. **Model constants ONLY use underscores** — Provider/Family/Harness keep PascalCase (no breaking change). Migration doc covers Model_* additions only.

### Open Items
- Curation review for AcceptabilityPreferred promotion — future epoch
- Full SBOM/PURL ecosystem integration (CycloneDX, OSV) — future epoch
- "Deprecated" rating assignments — future epoch
- Bidirectional HuggingFace Hub mapping — future epoch

### Acceptance Criteria

(See PROPOSAL-3 BDD section for full set; updated per UAT)

- ParseFamily handles 169 raw families + 30+ suffixes + override table + versioned variants (dotted: kimi-k2.5; hyphenated: claude-opus-4-5)
- ExtractDate matches only full YYYYMMDD or YYYY-MM-DD
- Resolve returns []ModelRef for cross-provider hosting, *ErrAmbiguous for multiple canonicals
- ModelRef has 6 fields including ID
- Format(SchemeRaw) returns the original API ID
- Format(SchemeHuggingFace) returns provider/raw-id format
- Format(SchemePURL) returns pkg:huggingface/provider/raw-id
- bestiary show accepts --scheme flag with auto-detect default
- bestiary-gen --no-fetch + cache miss → actionable error
- SQLite v2→v3 migration: 5 sub-tests pass (preserves-data, backfill, idempotent, edge-cases, index-used)
- Model constants follow Model_<Provider>_<Family>_<Variant>_<Date> underscore convention
- Provider/Family/Harness PascalCase preserved
- families_gen.go declares `type Family string` (named type)
- BestiarySchemaVersion exact string "0.0.2"
- bestiary.schema.json conformance test passes
- MIGRATION_v0.0.1_to_v0.0.2.md exists with all renames documented
- SQL migration file exists
- go vet ./... + CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./... pass

### Suggested Slice Decomposition

- **Slice 1**: parse package (parse.go, parse/data/*.json, ParseFamily/ExtractDate, tests). Foundation for everything else.
- **Slice 2**: ModelInfo extension + family methods + family.go. Bake parsed fields into static gen via codegen change.
- **Slice 3**: ModelRef + canonical.go + designation.go + Resolve + resolve.go. Public API.
- **Slice 4**: Model_ constants generation. Depends on parse + codegen baked fields.
- **Slice 5**: SQLite v2→v3 migration + QueryByCanonical + SQL migration file. Schema migration.
- **Slice 6**: CLI extension (`bestiary show` with --scheme) + cache flags (`bestiary-gen --cache-dir, --no-fetch`).
- **Slice 7**: bestiary.schema.json update + version bump + MIGRATION_v0.0.1_to_v0.0.2.md.
- **Slice 8**: Provenance companion PR (separate repo `dayvidpham/provenance`).

But supervisor decides final decomposition based on dependency analysis.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T09:44:16Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T09:44:16Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-2i0","title":"SLICE-6: CLI extensions — bestiary show --scheme + bestiary-gen --cache-dir/--no-fetch","description":"---
references:
  impl_plan: bestiary-0ip
  urd: bestiary-rjf
  proposal: bestiary-1oq
  handoff: bestiary-28c
  depends_on_slice: bestiary-99m
---

## Specification
Two orthogonal CLI changes bundled in one slice (single owner of cmd/* paths to avoid contention):

A. `bestiary show <input> [--scheme=<canonical|huggingface|purl|raw>]` — auto-detects scheme by prefix when flag absent. Resolves via Resolve(). Prints candidate table on *ErrAmbiguous.

B. `bestiary-gen [--cache-dir <path>] [--no-fetch]` — overrides hardcoded `.bestiary-gen-cache/`; `--no-fetch` reads cached api_response.json instead of HTTP. Cache miss with --no-fetch → actionable error per C-actionable-errors.

## Files Owned
- cmd/bestiary/main.go (add --scheme flag to show subcommand; replace direct LookupModel call with Resolve; render *ErrAmbiguous candidate table)
- cmd/bestiary/main_test.go (scheme tests; ambiguity rendering test)
- cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go (parseFlags additions; cache path threading; --no-fetch read-cache path)
- cmd/bestiary-gen/main_test.go (cache flag tests)
- format.go (helper for rendering ErrAmbiguous candidate table — reuse tableRow pattern)

## Auto-detect rules
| Input prefix | Scheme |
|---|---|
| `pkg:huggingface/` | SchemePURL |
| Contains `/` (and no `pkg:`) | SchemeHuggingFace |
| Default | SchemeCanonical (which falls through to raw lookup if no canonical match) |
| Last-resort fall-through | SchemeRaw — direct ModelID lookup |

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] `bestiary show claude-opus-4-20250514` returns the model.
- [ ] `bestiary show "anthropic/claude-opus-4-20250514"` (HuggingFace form) resolves.
- [ ] `bestiary show "pkg:huggingface/anthropic/claude-opus-4-20250514"` (PURL) resolves.
- [ ] `bestiary show claude --scheme=canonical` returns *ErrAmbiguous with Opus/Sonnet/Haiku candidates listed.
- [ ] `bestiary-gen --cache-dir /tmp/foo` writes to /tmp/foo/api_response.json.
- [ ] `bestiary-gen --no-fetch` with stale cache succeeds; with missing cache returns actionable error containing path + remediation.

## Acceptance Criteria (BDD)
- Given input "claude" When `bestiary show claude` Then exit code != 0 and stdout/stderr lists candidates from *ErrAmbiguous.
- Given input "pkg:huggingface/anthropic/claude-opus-4-20250514" When `bestiary show` Then output is the resolved model.
- Given a missing cache file When `bestiary-gen --no-fetch --cache-dir /nonexistent` Then exit != 0 and message tells the user (a) what file was expected, (b) why fetch was skipped (--no-fetch), (c) how to fix (drop --no-fetch or place a cached api_response.json at the path).

## Owner Worker
Wave 4, model: sonnet.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-25T09:57:01Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-25T09:57:01Z"}

## Specification
Public API surface for entity normalization. Refactor ModelRef to 6 fields (add ID), introduce CanonicalScheme + Format(scheme), Designation + AcceptabilityRating, and Resolve + *ErrAmbiguous. ModelRef.Ref() consumes ModelInfo.NormalizedFamily/Variant/Date populated by Slice 2.

## Files Owned
- modelref.go (refactor: ModelRef gains ID; Format(scheme) dispatch; Designations() method; Stringer uses SchemeCanonical)
- modelref_test.go (update for ID + Format dispatch)
- NEW canonical.go (CanonicalScheme int enum: SchemeCanonical, SchemeHuggingFace, SchemePURL, SchemeRaw)
- NEW canonical_test.go
- NEW designation.go (Designation struct + AcceptabilityRating int enum: Admitted, Preferred, Deprecated; all static designations default to Admitted)
- NEW designation_test.go
- NEW resolve.go (Resolve + ResolveOption + WithScheme)
- NEW resolve_test.go
- errors.go (add *ErrAmbiguous struct error)
- errors_test.go (cover ErrAmbiguous.Error() formatting)

## Cross-cutting
- ModelRef shape per UAT-1 Option A:
  - SchemeCanonical: `<provider>/<family>/<variant>@<date>` OR a provider's actual representation
  - SchemeHuggingFace: `<provider>/<raw-id>` (HF Hub form)
  - SchemePURL: `pkg:huggingface/<provider>/<raw-id>` (per ECMA-427 + purl-spec PR #201)
  - SchemeRaw: `string(r.ID)` — original API ID
- Resolve doc rule (Reviewer C-N1): cross-provider hosting (same Canonical, multiple Providers) → []ModelRef len>=1 with err=nil; multiple distinct Canonicals → *ErrAmbiguous; zero matches → ErrNotFound.
- Designations() on ModelRef returns admitted-only designations for now; rating promotion deferred to follow-up curation epoch.
- *ErrAmbiguous must be actionable: include input string, list of (canonical, provider, count) candidates, and remediation hint ("use --scheme=raw or refine input").

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] ModelRef has 6 fields: ID, Provider, RawFamily, Family, Variant, Date
- [ ] Format(SchemeRaw) returns string(r.ID)
- [ ] Format(SchemeHuggingFace) returns "<provider>/<raw-id>"
- [ ] Format(SchemePURL) returns "pkg:huggingface/<provider>/<raw-id>"
- [ ] Format(SchemeCanonical) returns either provider-specific or "<provider>/<family>/<variant>@<date>"
- [ ] Resolve splits []ModelRef vs *ErrAmbiguous correctly
- [ ] Designations() returns at least one Designation per ModelRef, all rated Admitted
- [ ] errors.As works on *ErrAmbiguous

## Acceptance Criteria (BDD)
- Given ModelRef{ID:"claude-opus-4-20250514"} When Format(SchemeRaw) Then returns "claude-opus-4-20250514".
- Given input "claude" matching distinct Canonicals (Opus, Sonnet, Haiku) When Resolve Then returns *ErrAmbiguous.
- Given Canonical "claude/opus@2025-05-14" hosted by anthropic, amazon-bedrock, azure When Resolve("claude-opus-4-20250514") Then returns []ModelRef of length 3, err=nil.
- Given any static ModelInfo When Designations() Then every Designation has Rating == Admitted.
