{"id":"bestiary-z2u7","title":"SLICE-FIX-V2-2: Resolve overhaul (PURL loose-fallback + ErrAmbiguous group/truncate + --format flag + canonical-provider preference)","description":"---
references:
  fix_plan_v2: bestiary-2xaf
  urd: bestiary-rjf
  uat: bestiary-g9ci
  followup_canonical_providers: bestiary-1wy7
  resolved_findings:
    - bestiary-e2uk  # extractCanonicalProviderHint stub — V2-2 fix #4 lands the impl
---
## Specification (Component 3 — UAT-3 REVISE)

Four required fixes to Resolve. Verbatim user responses captured in UAT-3 (`bestiary-g9ci`).

### Fix #1: PURL loose-match fallback (verbatim user response)
> "State that we are doing loose matching in the fallback. In zero match case, state that there are none and fallback to ErrAmbiguous with candidates"

When `Resolve('pkg:huggingface/<ns>/<id>')` has zero matches in namespace:
1. Message MUST state "no matches in namespace <ns>" (or equivalent — keep actionable per [C-actionable-errors])
2. Fall back to `ErrAmbiguous` with all-provider candidates (loose match)

Currently the namespace acts as a hard filter (resolve.go:198-200 strips prefix and uses providerHint as filter). Change behavior: when namespace filter yields 0, log/note the namespace miss and re-search without filter.

### Fix #2: ErrAmbiguous output (verbatim user response)
> "Should do both of these:
>   2. Truncate after N (e.g. 10) with '+M more' hint
>   3. Group by NormalizedFamily/Variant; show one canonical per group"

(Note: post-V2-1, this is grouping by Family/Variant/Version — the renamed fields.)

BOTH:
- (a) Truncate candidate list after N=10 with "+M more" hint
- (b) Group by (Family, Variant, Version) tuple — show ONE canonical row per group

This collapses the 17+ vercel-rehost rows currently shown for `claude` input into one canonical row per (Family, Variant, Version).

Implementation: ErrAmbiguous struct gains either a precomputed grouped+truncated view, or its formatter does the work. Formatter location is likely `format.go` (FormatAmbiguous, called from cmd/bestiary/main.go:122). Worker's call where the grouping logic lives (errors.go vs format.go), but tests must cover both grouping AND truncation behavior.

### Fix #3: NEW `--format` input flag (verbatim user response)
> "these `show` commands... Should only take our normalized representation by default. If we want to use HuggingFace format or PURL format or raw format, need to pass `--format {peasant (ours, default),huggingface,(also hf),purl,raw}` if we want to use others"

`bestiary show` defaults to canonical/peasant input form ONLY. Add `--format {peasant,huggingface|hf,purl,raw}` flag, default `peasant`.

`peasant` = bestiary canonical form (`provider/family/variant/version@date` per existing conventions).

The current Resolve auto-detect (resolve.go:191-236 `detectSchemeWithHint` — PURL via `pkg:` prefix, canonical via slashes) is currently always-on. New behavior:
- Resolve auto-detect path is REMOVED in favor of explicit `--format` selection from CLI
- When `--format` is supplied, Resolve dispatches directly to the matching scheme without auto-detect
- Default `peasant` means: parse input as canonical form ONLY; if it doesn't parse as canonical, return error (do NOT silently try other formats)

**Reconciliation note:** `cmd/bestiary/main.go:26-32` currently has both `--format` (json/yaml/table — OUTPUT format) and `--scheme` (resolve scheme override — INPUT format). User's verbatim ask is for `--format` to mean INPUT format. Worker MUST resolve this naming clash:
  - **Recommended:** rename existing OUTPUT `--format` flag to `--output` (or `-o`), and use `--format` for INPUT scheme as the user requested. Document the rename in MIGRATION doc.
  - **Alternative:** keep `--format` for output, add `--input-format` for input. (Less aligned with user's verbatim ask — flag name was specifically `--format`.)
  - Whichever path: surface the rename clearly in MIGRATION_v0.0.1_to_v0.0.2.md.

### Fix #4: NEW canonical-provider preference (verbatim user response)
> "Why this show provider 'qihang-ai' [in claude/opus@2025-11-01 result] — Anthropic should be the canonical provider here"

When canonical-form input matches multiple providers (e.g. Anthropic primary + 17 rehosts), Resolve must prefer the canonical/originating provider.

**Mechanism (locked by team-lead):**
- Add `func (f Family) CanonicalProvider() Provider` method in `family.go` (NOT in families_gen.go — that's auto-generated).
- Body is a static map (switch or map literal).
- Empty Provider on unknown family — Resolve falls back to ErrAmbiguous with all candidates (no panic).

**Wave 3 scope (verbatim user direction, UAT-3 Decision 2 catch-all):**
> "For now, we can just determine the canonical providers for the most popular models and stub the rest with a placeholder value, with a comment specifically stating that the placeholder will be reviewed and filled in during a followup."

Populate the well-known mappings:
- `claude` → `Anthropic`
- `gemini` → `Google`
- `gemma` → `Google`
- `gpt` → `OpenAI`
- `chatgpt` → `OpenAI`
- `o1`, `o3` → `OpenAI`
- `llama` → `Local` (per team-lead direction)
- Plus other clear cases the worker identifies (mistral, qwen, deepseek where confident — worker's call)

Unknowns: single sentinel return path with comment block:
```go
// TODO(followup bestiary-1wy7): review canonical provider for this family — see FOLLOWUP_SLICE-2
default:
    return "" // empty Provider; Resolve falls back to ErrAmbiguous
```

Placeholder MUST NOT be a wrong-but-plausible guess. Empty Provider only.

### Resolve flow change

When canonical-form input matches >1 provider:
1. Look up `Family.CanonicalProvider()`
2. If non-empty AND that provider is among match set → return single ModelRef from that provider
3. If empty OR not among matches → ErrAmbiguous with all candidates (existing behavior, but enhanced with Fix #2 grouping+truncation)

## Files Owned

- `resolve.go`: replace `extractCanonicalProviderHint` stub (lines 285-292) with real impl OR call `Family.CanonicalProvider()` from a new step in the Resolve dispatcher; PURL loose-fallback in providerHint filter logic (lines 87-92, 198-200)
- `family.go`: NEW `CanonicalProvider()` method with static map + TODO placeholder
- `errors.go`: ErrAmbiguous struct may gain grouped/truncated view (worker's call)
- `format.go`: `FormatAmbiguous` updated for grouping + truncation
- `cmd/bestiary/main.go`: `--format` input flag (lines 26-32 area), reconciliation with existing output flag
- `MIGRATION_v0.0.1_to_v0.0.2.md`: document --format flag, output-flag rename if any, canonical-provider behavior
- Tests: `resolve_test.go`, `format_test.go`, new tests for `Family.CanonicalProvider`, CLI integration tests

## Leaf Tasks
- SLICE-FIX-V2-2-L1: Types — Family.CanonicalProvider method skeleton; ErrAmbiguous shape changes if any; CLI flag wiring stub
- SLICE-FIX-V2-2-L2: Tests — TDD for all 4 fixes
- SLICE-FIX-V2-2-L3: Impl — Resolve dispatch changes, formatter grouping+truncation, --format flag handling

## Dependencies
- BLOCKED-BY V2-1: tests reference renamed ModelInfo fields; grouping is by renamed (Family, Variant, Version)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given `Resolve('pkg:huggingface/anthropic/<unknown-id>')` when no anthropic matches when there ARE matches under other providers then return ErrAmbiguous with message naming the missed namespace AND with all-provider candidates, should never silently return zero results without a namespace-miss diagnostic
- Given `Resolve('claude')` returning ErrAmbiguous when 17+ rehosts exist for same canonical (claude, opus, *) then candidate list shows ONE row per (Family, Variant, Version) tuple, should never return >1 row per canonical group
- Given an ErrAmbiguous candidate list with >10 distinct canonical groups when formatted then output truncates after 10 with "+M more" hint, should never dump a full unbounded list
- Given `bestiary show <input>` with no `--format` flag when input is canonical/peasant form then resolves cleanly, should never auto-detect PURL or HuggingFace
- Given `bestiary show pkg:huggingface/anthropic/claude-opus-4-5 --format purl` then resolves via PURL scheme, should never silently treat as canonical
- Given `Resolve('claude/opus@2025-11-01')` matching Anthropic + 17 rehosts when Family.CanonicalProvider returns Anthropic then return single Anthropic ModelRef, should never return qihang-ai or other rehost as primary
- Given an unknown family in CanonicalProvider when called then return empty Provider, should never panic; Resolve must fall back to ErrAmbiguous with all candidates
- Given Wave 3 stubs unknown families with TODO comment then `grep -n 'TODO(followup bestiary-1wy7)' family.go` matches; should never leave the placeholder uncommented or misreference the followup task

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] PURL namespace miss → loose-match fallback + diagnostic message
- [ ] ErrAmbiguous formatter groups by (Family, Variant, Version)
- [ ] ErrAmbiguous formatter truncates at 10 + "+M more"
- [ ] `--format {peasant,huggingface|hf,purl,raw}` flag added; default peasant
- [ ] Output flag clash with existing `--format` resolved (rename to `--output` recommended; documented in MIGRATION)
- [ ] `Family.CanonicalProvider()` method added in family.go
- [ ] Popular families populated; rest fall through to empty-Provider with TODO
- [ ] Resolve dispatcher uses CanonicalProvider on canonical-form multi-match
- [ ] `extractCanonicalProviderHint` stub removed/replaced (closes `bestiary-e2uk`)
- [ ] All tests pass; `go vet` clean
- [ ] Atomic commits per layer

{"id":"bestiary-wcvo","title":"SLICE-FIX-V2-3: Parse-failure audit log (.bestiary-gen-cache/parse_failures.json)","description":"---
references:
  fix_plan_v2: bestiary-2xaf
  urd: bestiary-rjf
  uat: bestiary-g9ci
---
## Specification (Component 5 — UAT-3 REVISE)

User verbatim:
> "Should record all parsing failures in a log file or DB table."

**Mechanism (locked by team-lead):** JSON file at `.bestiary-gen-cache/parse_failures.json` written by `bestiary-gen` at codegen time. Overwrites on each run (full audit per generation, not append).

NOT a SQLite table, NOT both, NOT stderr-only. SQLite is technically infeasible — bestiary-gen runs at build time with no store access; established by exploration.

## File envelope

```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "generated_at": "<RFC3339 timestamp>",
  "failure_count": <N>,
  "failures": [...]
}
```

## Per-record format

```json
{
  "raw_id": "claude-3-5-haiku-20241022",
  "provider": "anthropic",
  "raw_family": "claude-haiku",
  "attempted_parse": {
    "family": "claude",
    "variant": "haiku",
    "version": "",
    "date": "2024-10-22"
  },
  "reason": "version digits between family-prefix and variant not extracted"
}
```

## Failure detection

The parser currently never returns errors — overflow cases like `Model__302AI__Claude__3__5__Haiku__20241022` degrade silently. V2-3 must add explicit detection at parse time. Three known failure modes (use these as consistent reason phrasing):

1. "version digits between family-prefix and variant not extracted" — e.g. `claude-3-5-haiku-20241022` (3.x line)
2. "suffix overflow: extra segments after expected family/variant/version/date" — generic overflow case
3. "YYMM-date-as-version false-positive" — Mistral 2401, 2403 cases where 4-digit numerals are dates not versions

Worker may add other reason categories as discovered, but should aim for consistent phrasing.

## Files Owned

- `parse.go`: add explicit failure detection — return tuple of (parsed result, *FailureRecord) or accumulator pattern. Worker chooses idiomatic Go pattern (channel? slice accumulator on ParseFamily? new ParseFamilyDetailed entry point?)
- New type/struct for `FailureRecord` (likely in parse.go or new `parse_failures.go`)
- `cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go`: at end of `run()` — assemble failure list collected during `genToModelInfo()` (line 632 area) and write `{cacheDir}/parse_failures.json`
- Tests: `parse_test.go` for failure-record emission; `cmd/bestiary-gen/main_test.go` for file-write integration

## Constraints

- Output path uses `bestiary-gen`'s configured cache dir (default `.bestiary-gen-cache/`, overridable via existing `--cache-dir` flag from URD R7)
- Overwrites file on every codegen run — full audit, not append
- File MUST NOT be committed to repo (cache dir already gitignored — verify .gitignore)
- File MUST be valid JSON even when `failure_count == 0` (emit empty array, not omit the file)

## Leaf Tasks
- SLICE-FIX-V2-3-L1: Types — FailureRecord struct + JSON envelope type + parse.go API surface change
- SLICE-FIX-V2-3-L2: Tests — failure detection (each of 3 modes), file write integration, empty-failures envelope
- SLICE-FIX-V2-3-L3: Impl — parse.go failure accumulation, bestiary-gen file writer

## Dependencies
- BLOCKED-BY V2-1: parse.go references ModelInfo field naming for attempted_parse output (renamed Family/Variant/Version/Date fields)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given a model ID that fails parse cleanly when bestiary-gen runs then `.bestiary-gen-cache/parse_failures.json` contains one record per failure with all 5 required fields, should never silently swallow failures
- Given a fresh codegen run when zero failures occur then file contains `{"schema_version":1,...,"failure_count":0,"failures":[]}`, should never omit the file
- Given the existing 8000+ overflow-pattern Model__ constants when V2-3 lands then parse_failures.json captures the overflow cases with reason field, should never report "failure_count":0 if overflow exists
- Given `go test ./...` then all tests pass including new failure-detection unit tests, should never break existing parse tests

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] FailureRecord struct defined with JSON tags matching the per-record format
- [ ] Parse failure detection covers all 3 known failure modes
- [ ] bestiary-gen writes parse_failures.json at end of codegen run
- [ ] File overwrites cleanly on each run (no stale entries)
- [ ] Empty-failures case emits valid JSON envelope
- [ ] .gitignore excludes `.bestiary-gen-cache/parse_failures.json` (or whole cache dir)
- [ ] Tests cover detection + file write
- [ ] `CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./...` passes
- [ ] `go vet ./...` passes
- [ ] Atomic commits per layer

{"id":"bestiary-z2u7","title":"SLICE-FIX-V2-2: Resolve overhaul (PURL loose-fallback + ErrAmbiguous group/truncate + --format flag + canonical-provider preference)","description":"---
references:
  fix_plan_v2: bestiary-2xaf
  urd: bestiary-rjf
  uat: bestiary-g9ci
  followup_canonical_providers: bestiary-1wy7
  resolved_findings:
    - bestiary-e2uk  # extractCanonicalProviderHint stub — V2-2 fix #4 lands the impl
---
## Specification (Component 3 — UAT-3 REVISE)

Four required fixes to Resolve. Verbatim user responses captured in UAT-3 (`bestiary-g9ci`).

### Fix #1: PURL loose-match fallback (verbatim user response)
> "State that we are doing loose matching in the fallback. In zero match case, state that there are none and fallback to ErrAmbiguous with candidates"

When `Resolve('pkg:huggingface/<ns>/<id>')` has zero matches in namespace:
1. Message MUST state "no matches in namespace <ns>" (or equivalent — keep actionable per [C-actionable-errors])
2. Fall back to `ErrAmbiguous` with all-provider candidates (loose match)

Currently the namespace acts as a hard filter (resolve.go:198-200 strips prefix and uses providerHint as filter). Change behavior: when namespace filter yields 0, log/note the namespace miss and re-search without filter.

### Fix #2: ErrAmbiguous output (verbatim user response)
> "Should do both of these:
>   2. Truncate after N (e.g. 10) with '+M more' hint
>   3. Group by NormalizedFamily/Variant; show one canonical per group"

(Note: post-V2-1, this is grouping by Family/Variant/Version — the renamed fields.)

BOTH:
- (a) Truncate candidate list after N=10 with "+M more" hint
- (b) Group by (Family, Variant, Version) tuple — show ONE canonical row per group

This collapses the 17+ vercel-rehost rows currently shown for `claude` input into one canonical row per (Family, Variant, Version).

Implementation: ErrAmbiguous struct gains either a precomputed grouped+truncated view, or its formatter does the work. Formatter location is likely `format.go` (FormatAmbiguous, called from cmd/bestiary/main.go:122). Worker's call where the grouping logic lives (errors.go vs format.go), but tests must cover both grouping AND truncation behavior.

### Fix #3: NEW `--format` input flag (verbatim user response)
> "these `show` commands... Should only take our normalized representation by default. If we want to use HuggingFace format or PURL format or raw format, need to pass `--format {peasant (ours, default),huggingface,(also hf),purl,raw}` if we want to use others"

`bestiary show` defaults to canonical/peasant input form ONLY. Add `--format {peasant,huggingface|hf,purl,raw}` flag, default `peasant`.

`peasant` = bestiary canonical form (`provider/family/variant/version@date` per existing conventions).

The current Resolve auto-detect (resolve.go:191-236 `detectSchemeWithHint` — PURL via `pkg:` prefix, canonical via slashes) is currently always-on. New behavior:
- Resolve auto-detect path is REMOVED in favor of explicit `--format` selection from CLI
- When `--format` is supplied, Resolve dispatches directly to the matching scheme without auto-detect
- Default `peasant` means: parse input as canonical form ONLY; if it doesn't parse as canonical, return error (do NOT silently try other formats)

**Reconciliation note:** `cmd/bestiary/main.go:26-32` currently has both `--format` (json/yaml/table — OUTPUT format) and `--scheme` (resolve scheme override — INPUT format). User's verbatim ask is for `--format` to mean INPUT format. Worker MUST resolve this naming clash:
  - **Recommended:** rename existing OUTPUT `--format` flag to `--output` (or `-o`), and use `--format` for INPUT scheme as the user requested. Document the rename in MIGRATION doc.
  - **Alternative:** keep `--format` for output, add `--input-format` for input. (Less aligned with user's verbatim ask — flag name was specifically `--format`.)
  - Whichever path: surface the rename clearly in MIGRATION_v0.0.1_to_v0.0.2.md.

### Fix #4: NEW canonical-provider preference (verbatim user response)
> "Why this show provider 'qihang-ai' [in claude/opus@2025-11-01 result] — Anthropic should be the canonical provider here"

When canonical-form input matches multiple providers (e.g. Anthropic primary + 17 rehosts), Resolve must prefer the canonical/originating provider.

**Mechanism (locked by team-lead):**
- Add `func (f Family) CanonicalProvider() Provider` method in `family.go` (NOT in families_gen.go — that's auto-generated).
- Body is a static map (switch or map literal).
- Empty Provider on unknown family — Resolve falls back to ErrAmbiguous with all candidates (no panic).

**Wave 3 scope (verbatim user direction, UAT-3 Decision 2 catch-all):**
> "For now, we can just determine the canonical providers for the most popular models and stub the rest with a placeholder value, with a comment specifically stating that the placeholder will be reviewed and filled in during a followup."

Populate the well-known mappings:
- `claude` → `Anthropic`
- `gemini` → `Google`
- `gemma` → `Google`
- `gpt` → `OpenAI`
- `chatgpt` → `OpenAI`
- `o1`, `o3` → `OpenAI`
- `llama` → `Local` (per team-lead direction)
- Plus other clear cases the worker identifies (mistral, qwen, deepseek where confident — worker's call)

Unknowns: single sentinel return path with comment block:
```go
// TODO(followup bestiary-1wy7): review canonical provider for this family — see FOLLOWUP_SLICE-2
default:
    return "" // empty Provider; Resolve falls back to ErrAmbiguous
```

Placeholder MUST NOT be a wrong-but-plausible guess. Empty Provider only.

### Resolve flow change

When canonical-form input matches >1 provider:
1. Look up `Family.CanonicalProvider()`
2. If non-empty AND that provider is among match set → return single ModelRef from that provider
3. If empty OR not among matches → ErrAmbiguous with all candidates (existing behavior, but enhanced with Fix #2 grouping+truncation)

## Files Owned

- `resolve.go`: replace `extractCanonicalProviderHint` stub (lines 285-292) with real impl OR call `Family.CanonicalProvider()` from a new step in the Resolve dispatcher; PURL loose-fallback in providerHint filter logic (lines 87-92, 198-200)
- `family.go`: NEW `CanonicalProvider()` method with static map + TODO placeholder
- `errors.go`: ErrAmbiguous struct may gain grouped/truncated view (worker's call)
- `format.go`: `FormatAmbiguous` updated for grouping + truncation
- `cmd/bestiary/main.go`: `--format` input flag (lines 26-32 area), reconciliation with existing output flag
- `MIGRATION_v0.0.1_to_v0.0.2.md`: document --format flag, output-flag rename if any, canonical-provider behavior
- Tests: `resolve_test.go`, `format_test.go`, new tests for `Family.CanonicalProvider`, CLI integration tests

## Leaf Tasks
- SLICE-FIX-V2-2-L1: Types — Family.CanonicalProvider method skeleton; ErrAmbiguous shape changes if any; CLI flag wiring stub
- SLICE-FIX-V2-2-L2: Tests — TDD for all 4 fixes
- SLICE-FIX-V2-2-L3: Impl — Resolve dispatch changes, formatter grouping+truncation, --format flag handling

## Dependencies
- BLOCKED-BY V2-1: tests reference renamed ModelInfo fields; grouping is by renamed (Family, Variant, Version)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given `Resolve('pkg:huggingface/anthropic/<unknown-id>')` when no anthropic matches when there ARE matches under other providers then return ErrAmbiguous with message naming the missed namespace AND with all-provider candidates, should never silently return zero results without a namespace-miss diagnostic
- Given `Resolve('claude')` returning ErrAmbiguous when 17+ rehosts exist for same canonical (claude, opus, *) then candidate list shows ONE row per (Family, Variant, Version) tuple, should never return >1 row per canonical group
- Given an ErrAmbiguous candidate list with >10 distinct canonical groups when formatted then output truncates after 10 with "+M more" hint, should never dump a full unbounded list
- Given `bestiary show <input>` with no `--format` flag when input is canonical/peasant form then resolves cleanly, should never auto-detect PURL or HuggingFace
- Given `bestiary show pkg:huggingface/anthropic/claude-opus-4-5 --format purl` then resolves via PURL scheme, should never silently treat as canonical
- Given `Resolve('claude/opus@2025-11-01')` matching Anthropic + 17 rehosts when Family.CanonicalProvider returns Anthropic then return single Anthropic ModelRef, should never return qihang-ai or other rehost as primary
- Given an unknown family in CanonicalProvider when called then return empty Provider, should never panic; Resolve must fall back to ErrAmbiguous with all candidates
- Given Wave 3 stubs unknown families with TODO comment then `grep -n 'TODO(followup bestiary-1wy7)' family.go` matches; should never leave the placeholder uncommented or misreference the followup task

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] PURL namespace miss → loose-match fallback + diagnostic message
- [ ] ErrAmbiguous formatter groups by (Family, Variant, Version)
- [ ] ErrAmbiguous formatter truncates at 10 + "+M more"
- [ ] `--format {peasant,huggingface|hf,purl,raw}` flag added; default peasant
- [ ] Output flag clash with existing `--format` resolved (rename to `--output` recommended; documented in MIGRATION)
- [ ] `Family.CanonicalProvider()` method added in family.go
- [ ] Popular families populated; rest fall through to empty-Provider with TODO
- [ ] Resolve dispatcher uses CanonicalProvider on canonical-form multi-match
- [ ] `extractCanonicalProviderHint` stub removed/replaced (closes `bestiary-e2uk`)
- [ ] All tests pass; `go vet` clean
- [ ] Atomic commits per layer

## Scope (Layer 2: Tests written before impl, will fail until L3)

Add tests covering all 4 Resolve fixes. Use existing resolve_test.go and format_test.go conventions; add new test files where helpful.

### Fix #1: PURL loose-fallback
- Given `Resolve('pkg:huggingface/anthropic/<id-only-on-other-providers>')` then ErrAmbiguous returned with all-provider candidates AND error message names the missed namespace
- Given `Resolve('pkg:huggingface/anthropic/claude-opus-4-5')` (matching Anthropic) then returns Anthropic ModelRef cleanly (existing behavior preserved)

### Fix #2: ErrAmbiguous group + truncate
- Given input matching 17+ same-canonical rehosts then ErrAmbiguous candidate list has ONE row per (Family, Variant, Version)
- Given >10 distinct canonical groups then output truncates after 10 with "+M more" hint
- Given exactly 10 groups then no truncation hint emitted

### Fix #3: --format flag
- Given `bestiary show <canonical-input>` (default) then resolves as canonical/peasant
- Given `bestiary show <purl-input>` (default, no --format) then ERROR (no auto-detect)
- Given `bestiary show <purl-input> --format purl` then resolves via PURL
- Given `bestiary show <hf-input> --format huggingface` (and `--format hf`) then resolves via HF
- Given `bestiary show <raw-id> --format raw` then resolves via raw ID exact match

### Fix #4: Family.CanonicalProvider preference
- Given `Family.CanonicalProvider()` for "claude" then returns Anthropic; for "gemini" Google; for "gpt" OpenAI; for "llama" Local
- Given an unknown family then returns empty Provider, should never panic
- Given `Resolve('claude/opus@2025-11-01')` matching Anthropic + 17 rehosts then returns single Anthropic ModelRef
- Given canonical-form input where CanonicalProvider returns empty and >1 provider matches then ErrAmbiguous returned (no preference applied)

- format_test.go (Fix #2 formatter)

- cmd/bestiary/main_test.go or new CLI integration test (Fix #3)

- Given `CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./...` after L2 alone then tests COMPILE but FAIL — expected
- New test count: estimate ~15-25 new test functions/cases","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:23:56Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:23:56Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-7mqk","title":"SLICE-FIX-V2-3-L3: Impl — parse failure accumulation + bestiary-gen file writer","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-wcvo
  fix_plan_v2: bestiary-2xaf
---
## Scope (Layer 3: Impl)

### parse.go
- Detect failure at parse time for the 3 known modes:
  1. Version digits trapped between family-prefix and variant (3.x line, possibly more)
  2. Suffix overflow (>4 expected segments)
  3. YYMM-date-as-version false-positive (Mistral)
- Accumulate failures via the API surface chosen in L1
- Existing behavior preserved: parser still returns best-effort result; failure is annotation-only

### cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go
- During `run()`/`genToModelInfo` (line 632 area): collect failures
- At end of run, write `{cacheDir}/parse_failures.json` via `os.WriteFile` with formatted JSON (use `json.MarshalIndent` for readability)
- Handle write errors per [C-actionable-errors] — message must say where the file lives, why it failed, and how to recover

### .gitignore
- Verify `.bestiary-gen-cache/` is gitignored (it should already be)
- If `parse_failures.json` lives elsewhere, ensure it's gitignored

## Acceptance
- Given `CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./...` then all L2 tests pass
- Given a fresh `go generate ./...` run then `.bestiary-gen-cache/parse_failures.json` is generated and contains expected overflow cases (8000+ existing Model__ overflow cases produce a substantial failure list)
- Given `go vet ./...` clean

## Atomic Commits
- Commit 1: parse.go failure detection

## Scope (Layer 3: Impl)

### parse.go
- Detect failure at parse time for the 3 known modes:
  1. Version digits trapped between family-prefix and variant (3.x line, possibly more)
  2. Suffix overflow (>4 expected segments)
  3. YYMM-date-as-version false-positive (Mistral)
- Accumulate failures via the API surface chosen in L1
- Existing behavior preserved: parser still returns best-effort result; failure is annotation-only

### cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go
- During `run()`/`genToModelInfo` (line 632 area): collect failures
- At end of run, write `{cacheDir}/parse_failures.json` via `os.WriteFile` with formatted JSON (use `json.MarshalIndent` for readability)
- Handle write errors per [C-actionable-errors] — message must say where the file lives, why it failed, and how to recover

### .gitignore
- Verify `.bestiary-gen-cache/` is gitignored (it should already be)
- If `parse_failures.json` lives elsewhere, ensure it's gitignored

## Acceptance
- Given `CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./...` then all L2 tests pass
- Given a fresh `go generate ./...` run then `.bestiary-gen-cache/parse_failures.json` is generated and contains expected overflow cases (8000+ existing Model__ overflow cases produce a substantial failure list)
- Given `go vet ./...` clean

- Commit 1: parse.go failure detection
- Commit 2: cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go writer + .gitignore audit

{"id":"bestiary-r7uj","title":"SLICE-FIX-V2-2-L2: Tests — TDD for all 4 Resolve fixes","description":"---
references:
  slice: bestiary-z2u7
  fix_plan_v2: bestiary-2xaf
---
## Scope (Layer 2: Tests written before impl, will fail until L3)

Add tests covering all 4 Resolve fixes. Use existing resolve_test.go and format_test.go conventions; add new test files where helpful.

### Fix #1: PURL loose-fallback
- Given `Resolve('pkg:huggingface/anthropic/<id-only-on-other-providers>')` then ErrAmbiguous returned with all-provider candidates AND error message names the missed namespace
- Given `Resolve('pkg:huggingface/anthropic/claude-opus-4-5')` (matching Anthropic) then returns Anthropic ModelRef cleanly (existing behavior preserved)

### Fix #2: ErrAmbiguous group + truncate
- Given input matching 17+ same-canonical rehosts then ErrAmbiguous candidate list has ONE row per (Family, Variant, Version)
- Given >10 distinct canonical groups then output truncates after 10 with "+M more" hint
- Given exactly 10 groups then no truncation hint emitted

### Fix #3: --format flag
- Given `bestiary show <canonical-input>` (default) then resolves as canonical/peasant
- Given `bestiary show <purl-input>` (default, no --format) then ERROR (no auto-detect)
- Given `bestiary show <purl-input> --format purl` then resolves via PURL
- Given `bestiary show <hf-input> --format huggingface` (and `--format hf`) then resolves via HF
- Given `bestiary show <raw-id> --format raw` then resolves via raw ID exact match

### Fix #4: Family.CanonicalProvider preference
- Given `Family.CanonicalProvider()` for "claude" then returns Anthropic; for "gemini" Google; for "gpt" OpenAI; for "llama" Local
- Given an unknown family then returns empty Provider, should never panic
- Given `Resolve('claude/opus@2025-11-01')` matching Anthropic + 17 rehosts then returns single Anthropic ModelRef
- Given canonical-form input where CanonicalProvider returns empty and >1 provider matches then ErrAmbiguous returned (no preference applied)

## Files Owned
- resolve_test.go (Fix #1, #2, #4 dispatcher behavior)
- format_test.go (Fix #2 formatter)
- family_test.go (Fix #4 method tests, new file likely)
- cmd/bestiary/main_test.go or new CLI integration test (Fix #3)

## Acceptance
- Given `CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./...` after L2 alone then tests COMPILE but FAIL — expected
- New test count: estimate ~15-25 new test functions/cases","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:23:56Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:11:01Z"}

## Scope (Layer 3: Impl, makes L2 tests pass)

### resolve.go
- **Fix #1 PURL loose-fallback:**
  - Around line 198-200 (PURL detect path) and lines 87-92 (providerHint filter): when providerHint filter yields 0 results, broaden to all-provider candidates AND construct ErrAmbiguous with diagnostic message naming the missed namespace.
- **Fix #4 canonical-provider preference:**
  - Replace `extractCanonicalProviderHint` stub at lines 285-292 (CLOSES `bestiary-e2uk`)
  - When canonical-form input matches >1 provider: look up `Family.CanonicalProvider()`; if non-empty AND in match set, return single ref from that provider; else fall through to ErrAmbiguous.
- **--format flag dispatch:** explicit scheme dispatch instead of detectSchemeWithHint auto-detect when `--format` is supplied. Default `peasant` rejects PURL/HF inputs that aren't canonical.

### family.go
Populate the static map per team-lead direction:
```go
case "claude":
    return ProviderAnthropic
case "gemini", "gemma":
    return ProviderGoogle
case "gpt", "chatgpt", "o1", "o3":
    return ProviderOpenAI
case "llama":
    return ProviderLocal
// + worker-identified clear cases (mistral, qwen, deepseek where confident)
default:
    // TODO(followup bestiary-1wy7): review canonical provider for this family — see FOLLOWUP_SLICE-2
    return ""
```

Worker MUST consult `providers_gen.go` for exact constant names.

### format.go
- `FormatAmbiguous` (called from cmd/bestiary/main.go:122): group candidates by (Family, Variant, Version) → one row per group; truncate after N=10 with "+M more" hint.

### errors.go
- If ErrAmbiguous shape changed in L1, finalize the impl here.

### cmd/bestiary/main.go
- Wire `--format {peasant,huggingface|hf,purl,raw}` flag through to Resolve via ResolveOption
- Reconcile output flag clash: rename existing OUTPUT `--format` to `--output` (recommended). Update help text.

### MIGRATION_v0.0.1_to_v0.0.2.md
- Document new `--format` INPUT flag
- Document output flag rename if applied
- Document canonical-provider preference behavior + the `bestiary-1wy7` followup
- Document ErrAmbiguous output change (grouping + truncation)

## Acceptance
- Given `CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./...` then all L2 tests pass
- Given `go vet ./...` clean
- Given `grep -n 'extractCanonicalProviderHint' resolve.go` then either zero matches (function removed) or stub replaced with real impl (no "future enhancement" comment)
- Given `grep -n 'TODO(followup bestiary-1wy7)' family.go` matches at the placeholder default branch

## Atomic Commits
- Commit 1: family.go (CanonicalProvider populated)
- Commit 2: resolve.go (Fix #1 + #4 dispatcher)
- Commit 3: errors.go + format.go (Fix #2 grouping/truncate)
- Commit 4: cmd/bestiary/main.go (--format flag + output rename)
- Commit 5: MIGRATION docs

{"id":"bestiary-xopj","title":"SLICE-FIX-V2-5: Modifier field on ModelRef + ModelInfo (parser, canonical bracket-suffix, codegen template)","description":"---
references:
  fix_plan_v2: bestiary-2xaf
  urd: bestiary-rjf
  uat: bestiary-g9ci
  reverses_decision:
    note: Originally deferred to FOLLOWUP_SLICE-1 (bestiary-wi36). User reversed in UAT-3 supplementary clarification.
---
## Why this exists

User reversed prior "defer modifier extraction to FOLLOWUP_SLICE-1" decision (UAT-3 cycle 1 supplementary clarification, 2026-04-26).

Verbatim: "Yes — add a 'flavor' field for known suffixes" + renaming directive: **the field is called `Modifier`, not `Flavor`**.

Wave 3 scope expansion (post V2-1/V2-2/V2-4 close, V2-3 cycle 2 in flight). V2-5 is NEW — does not block any prior closed slice.

## Scope

Wave 3's parser currently emits parse-failure entries for trailing modifier tokens (e.g. `-thinking`). Instead of auditing these as failures, V2-5 extracts the modifier into a first-class field on ModelRef and ModelInfo, and threads it through canonical formatting and codegen.

## Public type changes

### ModelRef (7 → 8 fields)
Add `Modifier string` field. Worker chooses placement (after Version vs after Date — recommend AFTER Date so the canonical-key tuple Version/Date stays adjacent).

### ModelInfo
Add `Modifier string` field. Per V2-1 convention: just `Modifier` (NOT `NormalizedModifier`).

### bestiary.schema.json
Document the new field for both ModelInfo and $defs/ModelRef.

### version.go
Schema version stays `0.0.2` (additive change, consistent with prior wave).

## Parser changes (parse.go)

New function:
```go
// ExtractModifier returns the modifier suffix and the substring it consumed.
// modifier is empty if no known modifier suffix is present.
// modifierConsumed is the substring of id that ExtractModifier removed,
// so callers can pass the cleaned ID to date/version extractors without
// the modifier polluting heuristics.
func ExtractModifier(id ModelID, family Family, variant string) (modifier string, modifierConsumed string)
```

`ParseFamilyWithVersion` (or its caller in `genToModelInfo` / `ParseFamilyDetailed`) calls ExtractModifier AFTER variant resolution but BEFORE date/version extraction. Order:
1. ParseFamily (raw → family + variant)
2. ExtractModifier (id, family, variant) → modifier, consumed
3. Strip consumed from id
4. ExtractVersionFromID on cleaned id
5. ExtractDate on cleaned id

## New data file: `parse/data/modifiers.json`

Follows existing `parse/data/*.json` convention (`_comment` field + structured object).

```json
{
  "_comment": "Known modifier suffixes for model IDs. ExtractModifier consults this list to extract the modifier as a separate field rather than treating it as suffix overflow. Add new modifiers here as they appear in the wild.",
  "schema_version": 1,
  "modifiers": [
    "thinking",
    "think",
    "vision",
    "latest",
    "code",
    "preview"
  ]
}
```

Worker may add others observed in the codegen audit (Doubao, Mistral, etc.) — document seed-vs-discovered split in commit message.

## Format changes (USER DECIDED)

### Canonical bracket-suffix
```
anthropic/claude/opus/4.6@2026-02-05[thinking]
```

If `Modifier` is empty, brackets omitted (preserves current form):
```
anthropic/claude/opus/4.5@2025-11-01
```

`canonical.go` updates:
- Format dispatcher emits `[modifier]` suffix when `ref.Modifier != ""`
- Parse dispatcher recognizes and consumes `[...]` suffix into `Modifier`
- Round-trip: `Resolve('anthropic/claude/opus/4.6@2026-02-05[thinking]')` works

`format.go` table renderer + JSON/YAML serializers: emit `Modifier` when non-empty.

## Codegen changes (USER DECIDED)

### Constant naming with __Modifier__ slot

Modifier slots between version and date:
```
Model__Anthropic__Claude__Opus__4_6__Thinking__20260205
Model__302AI__Claude__Opus__4_6__Thinking         (no date — modifier trailing)
Model__Anthropic__Claude__Opus__4_5__20251101     (no modifier — preserves current form)
```

`cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go`:
- Update constant-name template
- Apply ChatGPT/AI casing override to modifier tokens (e.g. `thinking` → `Thinking`, `vision` → `Vision`)
- Full regen of `models_constants_gen.go` and `models_static_gen.go`

## Test corpus (per team-lead)

- `claude-opus-4-1-20250805-thinking` → Family=claude, Variant=opus, Version=4.1, Date=2025-08-05, Modifier=thinking
- `claude-opus-4-6-thinking` → Family=claude, Variant=opus, Version=4.6, Date="", Modifier=thinking
- `doubao-seed-1-6-thinking-250715` → Family=doubao, Variant=seed, Version=1.6, Date=??, Modifier=thinking (250715 YYMMDD without dashes is separate concern; expected Date or audit-log entry — worker's call)
- `gpt-4o-2024-05-13` → no modifier; ensure bracket-suffix doesn't fire when Modifier empty

## Files Owned

- `bestiary.go` (ModelInfo struct add Modifier field + doc)
- `modelref.go` (ModelRef struct add Modifier field + Ref() method update)
- `bestiary.schema.json` (Modifier property + required array)
- `parse.go` (NEW ExtractModifier + integration into parse pipeline + load modifiers.json)
- NEW `parse/data/modifiers.json`
- `canonical.go` (Format + Parse for bracket-suffix)
- `format.go` (table/JSON/YAML emission of Modifier)
- `store.go` (SQL schema migration v4 → v5? Add modifier column. Or: SQL columns NOT updated — Modifier lives in normalized struct only? Worker decides; prefer NOT changing SQL schema if Modifier is parser-derived from existing rawFamily/id columns)
- `cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go` (codegen template update + casing override application)
- `models_static_gen.go` (regen)
- `models_constants_gen.go` (regen)
- `MIGRATION_v0.0.1_to_v0.0.2.md` (NEW Section 13 — Modifier field + canonical bracket-suffix + Model__ slot)
- Tests: `parse_test.go`, `canonical_test.go`, `modelref_test.go`, `format_test.go`, `store_test.go` (if SQL changes), `cmd/bestiary-gen/main_test.go`

## Leaf Tasks
- SLICE-FIX-V2-5-L1: Types — ModelInfo + ModelRef + Ref() + schema additions; ExtractModifier signature; modifiers.json
- SLICE-FIX-V2-5-L2: Tests — TDD for ExtractModifier (4-case corpus + edge cases) + canonical bracket round-trip + Model__ constant template + JSON/YAML emission
- SLICE-FIX-V2-5-L3: Impl — ExtractModifier body + canonical Format/Parse + codegen template + casing override + regen + MIGRATION docs

## Dependencies

- Parallel with V2-3 cycle 2 (touches different code — V2-3 audit-log only, V2-5 extraction + types + canonical + codegen)
- V2-1 (closed) — Modifier field added consistent with V2-1's Drop-Normalized-prefix convention
- V2-2 (closed) — Bracket-suffix parsing extends V2-2's --format peasant; doesn't reopen V2-2

## Concurrency note

V2-3 cycle-2 worker and V2-5 worker BOTH touch `parse.go`:
- V2-3: Mode 2 redefinition (allowlist + reason constants, audit-log emission)
- V2-5: ExtractModifier function (extraction into Modifier field)

Mitigations: distinct sections of parse.go; atomic commits per layer; surface real conflicts via bd comments rather than overwrite.

V2-5's ExtractModifier will SHRINK V2-3's audit log (overflow cases for known modifiers become clean parses). V2-3 still has Mode 1 (version digits between family/variant) + ReasonUnknownSuffixOverflow (allowlist gap audit) + Mode 3 (YYMM false-positive) — its scope is preserved.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Given `claude-opus-4-1-20250805-thinking` when parsed then ModelInfo emits Modifier="thinking", Family="claude", Variant="opus", Version="4.1", Date="2025-08-05"
- Given `gpt-4o-2024-05-13` when parsed then Modifier="" and canonical String() emits NO bracket suffix
- Given `Resolve('anthropic/claude/opus/4.6@2026-02-05[thinking]')` then resolves cleanly with Modifier="thinking"
- Given codegen runs then `Model__Anthropic__Claude__Opus__4_6__Thinking__20260205` constant exists in `models_constants_gen.go`
- Given an unknown modifier (e.g. `-zen`) when parsed then Modifier="" and parse_failures.json (V2-3) emits ReasonUnknownSuffixOverflow
- Given `go test ./...` then all tests pass; `go vet` clean
- Given `bestiary.schema.json` validation against ModelInfo JSON output then validates with new Modifier field

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] ModelRef field added, Ref() updated
- [ ] ModelInfo field added
- [ ] bestiary.schema.json updated, schema_test.go passes
- [ ] parse/data/modifiers.json created with seed + observed values
- [ ] ExtractModifier function with documented signature
- [ ] Parse pipeline order: ParseFamily → ExtractModifier → strip → ExtractVersion → ExtractDate
- [ ] canonical.go Format emits bracket-suffix (when non-empty)
- [ ] canonical.go Parse consumes bracket-suffix
- [ ] format.go JSON/YAML/table emit Modifier
- [ ] codegen template emits __Modifier__ slot
- [ ] Casing override applied to modifier tokens (Thinking, Vision, etc.)
- [ ] Full regen of models_constants_gen.go + models_static_gen.go
- [ ] MIGRATION Section 13 documents new field + bracket-suffix + Model__ slot
- [ ] Test corpus (4 cases) all pass
- [ ] Atomic commits per layer

## Why this exists

User reversed prior "defer modifier extraction to FOLLOWUP_SLICE-1" decision (UAT-3 cycle 1 supplementary clarification, 2026-04-26).

Verbatim: "Yes — add a 'flavor' field for known suffixes" + renaming directive: **the field is called `Modifier`, not `Flavor`**.

Wave 3 scope expansion (post V2-1/V2-2/V2-4 close, V2-3 cycle 2 in flight). V2-5 is NEW — does not block any prior closed slice.

## Scope

Wave 3's parser currently emits parse-failure entries for trailing modifier tokens (e.g. `-thinking`). Instead of auditing these as failures, V2-5 extracts the modifier into a first-class field on ModelRef and ModelInfo, and threads it through canonical formatting and codegen.

## Public type changes

### ModelRef (7 → 8 fields)
Add `Modifier string` field. Worker chooses placement (after Version vs after Date — recommend AFTER Date so the canonical-key tuple Version/Date stays adjacent).

### ModelInfo
Add `Modifier string` field. Per V2-1 convention: just `Modifier` (NOT `NormalizedModifier`).

### bestiary.schema.json
Document the new field for both ModelInfo and $defs/ModelRef.

### version.go
Schema version stays `0.0.2` (additive change, consistent with prior wave).

## Parser changes (parse.go)

New function:
```go
// ExtractModifier returns the modifier suffix and the substring it consumed.
// modifier is empty if no known modifier suffix is present.
// modifierConsumed is the substring of id that ExtractModifier removed,
// so callers can pass the cleaned ID to date/version extractors without
// the modifier polluting heuristics.
func ExtractModifier(id ModelID, family Family, variant string) (modifier string, modifierConsumed string)
```

`ParseFamilyWithVersion` (or its caller in `genToModelInfo` / `ParseFamilyDetailed`) calls ExtractModifier AFTER variant resolution but BEFORE date/version extraction. Order:
1. ParseFamily (raw → family + variant)
2. ExtractModifier (id, family, variant) → modifier, consumed
3. Strip consumed from id
4. ExtractVersionFromID on cleaned id
5. ExtractDate on cleaned id

## New data file: `parse/data/modifiers.json`

Follows existing `parse/data/*.json` convention (`_comment` field + structured object).

```json
{
  "_comment": "Known modifier suffixes for model IDs. ExtractModifier consults this list to extract the modifier as a separate field rather than treating it as suffix overflow. Add new modifiers here as they appear in the wild.",
  "schema_version": 1,
  "modifiers": [
    "thinking",
    "think",
    "vision",
    "latest",
    "code",
    "preview"
  ]
}
```

Worker may add others observed in the codegen audit (Doubao, Mistral, etc.) — document seed-vs-discovered split in commit message.

## Format changes (USER DECIDED)

### Canonical bracket-suffix
```
anthropic/claude/opus/4.6@2026-02-05[thinking]
```

If `Modifier` is empty, brackets omitted (preserves current form):
```
anthropic/claude/opus/4.5@2025-11-01
```

`canonical.go` updates:
- Format dispatcher emits `[modifier]` suffix when `ref.Modifier != ""`
- Parse dispatcher recognizes and consumes `[...]` suffix into `Modifier`
- Round-trip: `Resolve('anthropic/claude/opus/4.6@2026-02-05[thinking]')` works

`format.go` table renderer + JSON/YAML serializers: emit `Modifier` when non-empty.

## Codegen changes (USER DECIDED)

### Constant naming with __Modifier__ slot

Modifier slots between version and date:
```
Model__Anthropic__Claude__Opus__4_6__Thinking__20260205
Model__302AI__Claude__Opus__4_6__Thinking         (no date — modifier trailing)
Model__Anthropic__Claude__Opus__4_5__20251101     (no modifier — preserves current form)
```

`cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go`:
- Update constant-name template
- Apply ChatGPT/AI casing override to modifier tokens (e.g. `thinking` → `Thinking`, `vision` → `Vision`)
- Full regen of `models_constants_gen.go` and `models_static_gen.go`

## Test corpus (per team-lead)

- `claude-opus-4-1-20250805-thinking` → Family=claude, Variant=opus, Version=4.1, Date=2025-08-05, Modifier=thinking
- `claude-opus-4-6-thinking` → Family=claude, Variant=opus, Version=4.6, Date="", Modifier=thinking
- `doubao-seed-1-6-thinking-250715` → Family=doubao, Variant=seed, Version=1.6, Date=??, Modifier=thinking (250715 YYMMDD without dashes is separate concern; expected Date or audit-log entry — worker's call)
- `gpt-4o-2024-05-13` → no modifier; ensure bracket-suffix doesn't fire when Modifier empty

- `modelref.go` (ModelRef struct add Modifier field + Ref() method update)
- `bestiary.schema.json` (Modifier property + required array)

- NEW `parse/data/modifiers.json`

- `format.go` (table/JSON/YAML emission of Modifier)
- `store.go` (SQL schema migration v4 → v5? Add modifier column. Or: SQL columns NOT updated — Modifier lives in normalized struct only? Worker decides; prefer NOT changing SQL schema if Modifier is parser-derived from existing rawFamily/id columns)
- `cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go` (codegen template update + casing override application)

- `models_constants_gen.go` (regen)

## Leaf Tasks

- SLICE-FIX-V2-5-L2: Tests — TDD for ExtractModifier (4-case corpus + edge cases) + canonical bracket round-trip + Model__ constant template + JSON/YAML emission

- V2-2 (closed) — Bracket-suffix parsing extends V2-2's --format peasant; doesn't reopen V2-2

## Concurrency note

V2-3 cycle-2 worker and V2-5 worker BOTH touch `parse.go`:
- V2-3: Mode 2 redefinition (allowlist + reason constants, audit-log emission)
- V2-5: ExtractModifier function (extraction into Modifier field)

Mitigations: distinct sections of parse.go; atomic commits per layer; surface real conflicts via bd comments rather than overwrite.

V2-5's ExtractModifier will SHRINK V2-3's audit log (overflow cases for known modifiers become clean parses). V2-3 still has Mode 1 (version digits between family/variant) + ReasonUnknownSuffixOverflow (allowlist gap audit) + Mode 3 (YYMM false-positive) — its scope is preserved.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Given `claude-opus-4-1-20250805-thinking` when parsed then ModelInfo emits Modifier="thinking", Family="claude", Variant="opus", Version="4.1", Date="2025-08-05"

- Given an unknown modifier (e.g. `-zen`) when parsed then Modifier="" and parse_failures.json (V2-3) emits ReasonUnknownSuffixOverflow
- Given `go test ./...` then all tests pass; `go vet` clean
- Given `bestiary.schema.json` validation against ModelInfo JSON output then validates with new Modifier field

- [ ] canonical.go Format emits bracket-suffix (when non-empty)
- [ ] canonical.go Parse consumes bracket-suffix

{"description":"## Finding

Resolve('anthropic/claude/opus/4.6@2026-02-05[thinking]') returns ErrNotFound (not-found error).

Tested: wrote temporary test TestResolveBracketSuffix which confirmed:
  resolve_bracket_test.go:10: Resolve returned error: bestiary: model not found: anthropic/claude/opus/4.6@2026-02-05[thinking]

## Root cause

matchCanonicalSegments in resolve.go strips '@date' suffix but does NOT strip the '[modifier]' bracket-suffix before segment splitting. The bracket '[thinking]' remains appended to the date segment, causing dateFilter = '2026-02-05[thinking]' which fails to match any model's Date field ('2026-02-05').

## Acceptance criterion

Explicitly stated in slice spec:
  'Given Resolve("anthropic/claude/opus/4.6@2026-02-05[thinking]") then resolves cleanly with Modifier="thinking"'

## Worker statement

Worker explicitly deferred: 'canonical.go Parse path for bracket-suffix NOT implemented... output-only in this slice per acceptance criteria.'

This contradicts the slice acceptance criteria. The acceptance criteria is the authoritative requirement; the worker's claim it was 'out of scope' is incorrect.

## Fix required

matchCanonicalSegments must strip the '[modifier]' bracket-suffix after stripping '@date', and optionally use it to filter by Modifier if/when models carry a Modifier field. Specifically:
1. After stripping '@date', check if remaining string ends with ']'
2. If so, extract '[modifier]' bracket suffix for optional filter
3. Continue segment parsing on the cleaned string without the bracket suffix

This is a BLOCKER because the acceptance criterion is literally the third bullet in the validation checklist and is untestable without this parse path."}

{"description":"## What

`Resolve('anthropic/claude/opus/4.6@2026-02-05[thinking]')` returns `ErrNotFound`. The bracket-suffix parse path is NOT implemented in `matchCanonicalSegments` in `resolve.go`.

## Why this is a BLOCKER

The slice spec acceptance criteria explicitly required: "Given `Resolve('anthropic/claude/opus/4.6@2026-02-05[thinking]')` then resolves cleanly with Modifier=\"thinking\"". This is a round-trip requirement. The worker's NOTES admitted "canonical.go Parse path for bracket-suffix NOT implemented" but claimed it was "output-only per acceptance criteria" — this directly contradicts the acceptance criteria in bestiary-xopj.

## Evidence

```
$ go run brackettest/main.go
ERROR: bestiary: model not found: anthropic/claude/opus/4.6@2026-02-05[thinking]
```

`matchCanonicalSegments` in resolve.go (line ~491) strips `@date` suffix but does NOT strip `[modifier]` bracket suffix. The bracket remains as part of the date filter string, causing all matches to fail.

## Fix required

In `resolve.go`, `matchCanonicalSegments()`:
1. After stripping `@date` suffix, also strip `[modifier]` bracket suffix if present.
2. Store the stripped modifier string.
3. When all other filters match, additionally filter `m.Modifier == modifierFilter` (when non-empty).

Alternatively: strip `[modifier]` from the input string in the detect/normalize path before it reaches `matchCanonicalSegments`.

## Where

`/home/minttea/codebases/dayvidpham/bestiary/main/resolve.go`, function `matchCanonicalSegments` (~line 491)

## Test that must pass after fix

```go
refs, err := bestiary.Resolve("anthropic/claude/opus/4.6@2026-02-05[thinking]")
// err must be nil (or ErrAmbiguous, not ErrNotFound)
// refs[0].Modifier must be "thinking"
```"}

- V2-3: Mode 2 redefinition (allowlist + reason constants, audit-log emission)
- V2-5: ExtractModifier function (extraction into Modifier field)

Mitigations: distinct sections of parse.go; atomic commits per layer; surface real conflicts via bd comments rather than overwrite.

V2-5's ExtractModifier will SHRINK V2-3's audit log (overflow cases for known modifiers become clean parses). V2-3 still has Mode 1 (version digits between family/variant) + ReasonUnknownSuffixOverflow (allowlist gap audit) + Mode 3 (YYMM false-positive) — its scope is preserved.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Given `claude-opus-4-1-20250805-thinking` when parsed then ModelInfo emits Modifier="thinking", Family="claude", Variant="opus", Version="4.1", Date="2025-08-05"
- Given `gpt-4o-2024-05-13` when parsed then Modifier="" and canonical String() emits NO bracket suffix
- Given `Resolve('anthropic/claude/opus/4.6@2026-02-05[thinking]')` then resolves cleanly with Modifier="thinking"
- Given codegen runs then `Model__Anthropic__Claude__Opus__4_6__Thinking__20260205` constant exists in `models_constants_gen.go`
- Given an unknown modifier (e.g. `-zen`) when parsed then Modifier="" and parse_failures.json (V2-3) emits ReasonUnknownSuffixOverflow
- Given `go test ./...` then all tests pass; `go vet` clean
- Given `bestiary.schema.json` validation against ModelInfo JSON output then validates with new Modifier field

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] ModelRef field added, Ref() updated
- [ ] ModelInfo field added
- [ ] bestiary.schema.json updated, schema_test.go passes
- [ ] parse/data/modifiers.json created with seed + observed values
- [ ] ExtractModifier function with documented signature
- [ ] Parse pipeline order: ParseFamily → ExtractModifier → strip → ExtractVersion → ExtractDate
- [ ] canonical.go Format emits bracket-suffix (when non-empty)
- [ ] canonical.go Parse consumes bracket-suffix
- [ ] format.go JSON/YAML/table emit Modifier
- [ ] codegen template emits __Modifier__ slot
- [ ] Casing override applied to modifier tokens (Thinking, Vision, etc.)
- [ ] Full regen of models_constants_gen.go + models_static_gen.go
- [ ] MIGRATION Section 13 documents new field + bracket-suffix + Model__ slot
- [ ] Test corpus (4 cases) all pass
- [ ] Atomic commits per layer

references:
  followup_epic: bestiary-602y
  source_finding: bestiary-8wuo
  source_slice: bestiary-wcvo
---

## Finding

parse.go contains a hardcoded `knownModifierTokens` map (lines ~1026–1031) that duplicates the 6 modifier tokens (thinking, think, vision, latest, code, preview) seeded in `parse/data/modifiers.json` by SLICE-FIX-V2-5 (bestiary-xopj).

The comment in parse.go explicitly acknowledges the duplication:
> NOTE: this list is intentionally duplicated from the modifier allowlist that SLICE-FIX-V2-5 embeds in parse/data/modifiers.json. A follow-up task (filed under FOLLOWUP epic bestiary-602y) will refactor to share a single source-of-truth once V2-5 has landed.

V2-5 has now landed (CLOSED). Time to deduplicate.

## Scope

- Refactor parse.go Mode 2 KnownSuffixOverflow detection to consult the same loaded modifier set that ExtractModifier uses (parse/data/modifiers.json).
- Remove the inline `knownModifierTokens` hardcoded map.
- Ensure parity tests still pass (the 6 seed tokens must produce identical Mode 2 detection behavior after the refactor).
- No behavioral change intended — purely a deduplication refactor.

## Why this matters

Parity must currently be maintained manually. Any addition to modifiers.json that is not also added to knownModifierTokens (or vice versa) will create split-brain between V2-3 Mode 2 detection and V2-5 ExtractModifier.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Given a modifier token added to parse/data/modifiers.json when parser runs then both ExtractModifier and Mode 2 KnownSuffixOverflow detection use the new token, should never require updating two locations.
- Given the existing test suite when refactor lands then all parse tests still pass with no behavioral change to detection output.
- Given knownModifierTokens map when refactor lands then it is removed from parse.go.

## Related

- Source IMPORTANT finding: bestiary-8wuo (SLICE-FIX-V2-5 review-C-1)
- Originating slice: bestiary-wcvo (V2-3 cycle-2 introduced the duplication intentionally)
- Tracked under FOLLOWUP epic: bestiary-602y","title":"FOLLOWUP: Deduplicate parse.go knownModifierTokens with parse/data/modifiers.json (single source of truth)"}

{"id":"bestiary-0ybb","title":"SLICE-FIX-V3-1: ErrAmbiguous canonical-vs-rehost distinction + PURL missed-namespace note","description":"---
references:
  fix_plan_v3: bestiary-dftg
  uat: bestiary-2mrr
  urd: bestiary-rjf
---
## Specification
Two surgical fixes to Component 3 ambiguous-result rendering. Same file cluster.

### Fix 1 — Canonical-provider distinction in the ErrAmbiguous table
When a Family has a canonical provider (Family.CanonicalProvider() != ""), the
ambiguous output must surface that provider rows AND visually distinguish them
from rehosts. Two coordinated edits:

(a) resolve.go (~line 255-261, ambiguous candidate assembly): when picking the
    representative ModelRef per (Family,Variant,Date) group, PREFER the row whose
    Provider == ref.Family.CanonicalProvider() if such a row exists in that group;
    otherwise keep byGroup[key][0]. This makes Anthropic rows APPEAR as the
    representative wherever Anthropic hosts the model. Do NOT change the cross-provider
    single-group path (resolve.go:241-250) or the PURL loose-fallback candidate build.

(b) format.go FormatAmbiguous (~line 321): visually distinguish canonical rows.
    Recommended (worker has design latitude — justify your pick in a bd comment):
      - Sort canonical-provider rows to the TOP before truncation (so they survive
        the N=10 cut), AND
      - Add a visible marker distinguishing canonical from rehost — e.g. a 4th
        "canonical" column / a trailing "*" / a "(canonical)" tag. A row is canonical
        when row.Provider == row.Family.CanonicalProvider().
    Extend the EXISTING grouping (by Family,Variant,Version) and truncation
    (N=10 + "+M more") — do NOT rebuild them.

### Fix 2 — Surface the PURL loose-fallback missed-namespace note
In FormatAmbiguous, when e.PURLMissedNamespace != "", print a note BEFORE/ABOVE the
candidate table, matching the errors.go wording:
  "no matches in namespace %q — performing loose match across all providers"
Keep ErrAmbiguous.Error() behavior unchanged (both paths show it).

## Files Owned
- resolve.go (candidate-assembly representative selection only)
- format.go (FormatAmbiguous rendering)
- format_test.go (new/updated tests)
- resolve_test.go (new/updated tests if needed)

## Leaf Tasks
- SLICE-FIX-V3-1-L1: Tests (test-first) for canonical distinction + missed-namespace note
- SLICE-FIX-V3-1-L2: Impl — resolve.go representative preference + format.go marker/sort + note

## Validation Checklist
- [ ] `bestiary show claude` shows Anthropic rows, visually marked canonical
- [ ] Missed-namespace note printed by FormatAmbiguous
- [ ] PURL anthropic/claude-opus-4-5 still returns anthropic (no regression)
- [ ] claude/opus@2025-11-24 still returns Provider=anthropic (no regression)
- [ ] New tests cover both fixes
- [ ] CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./... and go vet ./... pass

## OUT OF SCOPE
- InferFamilyFromID / empty-RawFamily Modifier mangling (bestiary-4yuy FOLLOWUP)
- Parser core. If a fix seems to need parser changes, STOP and notify supervisor.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-05-30T04:42:41Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-05-30T04:42:41Z"}

## Scope
Write tests (test-first) that import the production code paths and FAIL until L2 impl.

### Test 1 — ambiguous-table canonical distinction (format_test.go)
Build an *ErrAmbiguous whose Candidates mix the canonical provider (e.g. Family=claude,
Provider=anthropic) with rehosts (deepinfra, nano-gpt). Assert FormatAmbiguous output:
  - includes at least one anthropic (canonical) row, and
  - visually marks/distinguishes the canonical row(s) from rehosts (assert on the
    chosen marker/section/column), and
  - canonical rows are not truncated away (place enough rehost groups to exceed N=10).
Use Family.CanonicalProvider() so the test tracks the real mapping (claude->anthropic).

### Test 2 — missed-namespace note (format_test.go)
Build an *ErrAmbiguous with PURLMissedNamespace="nonexistent". Assert FormatAmbiguous
output contains a note like `no matches in namespace "nonexistent"`. Add a negative
case: when PURLMissedNamespace=="" the note is absent.

### Test 3 (optional, resolve_test.go) — representative preference
If feasible at unit level, assert that for a bare-family ambiguous resolve the
representative ModelRef for a group hosted by the canonical provider has
Provider == CanonicalProvider().

## Files Owned
- format_test.go (helper makeAmbiguousRefs exists at line ~386 — reuse/extend)
- resolve_test.go (if adding representative-preference test)

## Acceptance Criteria
Given the two fixes when tests run before L2 then they FAIL (red); should never
pass against the current code. Tests must use stdlib testing only (no testify).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-05-30T04:42:56Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-05-30T04:42:56Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-9lnq","title":"Non-deterministic codegen: models slice unsorted → unstable Model__*_N collision suffixes + struct order","description":"Discovered post-v0.2.0 while verifying generated-code freshness. Re-running 'go run ./cmd/bestiary-gen --no-fetch' against the SAME cached api_response.json produces a content-identical but reordered output: models_static_gen.go shows 75111/75111 equal insert/delete (full struct reshuffle), and models_constants_gen.go shows ~28 collision-suffix swaps where e.g. Model__AlibabaCN__Kimi__K2__5_1 flips from 'kimi/kimi-k2.5' to 'kimi-k2.5'.

Root cause: the constants file IS sorted by constName at emit (cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go ~line 1680), so constant NAMES are stable. But the _1/_2 collision-disambiguation suffix is assigned earlier, in the order of the 'models' slice, and that slice is not deterministically ordered (upstream map-iteration order). Same unsorted slice also determines models_static_gen.go struct emission order.

Impact:
- go generate is not reproducible (always shows a large diff).
- Model__*_N numbered collision constants are NOT a stable reference across regens — _1 vs _2 can point to different raw IDs run-to-run. Correctness hazard for consumers of those specific numbered constants.
- The committed v0.2.0 generated files are content-correct and internally self-consistent; this manifests on the NEXT regen, not in the shipped release.

Fix: sort 'models []ModelInfo' by (Provider, ID) once, immediately after assembly, before genToModelInfo struct emission AND before collision-suffix assignment. Then regen once (deterministically) and commit — expect a one-time large but semantically-empty diff. Add a test asserting two successive --no-fetch regens are byte-identical.

Severity: IMPORTANT (reproducible builds + stable public API), not a release blocker.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"dayvidpham@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-05-30T06:44:02Z","created_by":"David Huu Pham","updated_at":"2026-05-30T06:44:02Z"}

{"id":"bestiary-bs08","title":"SLICE-DET-1-L1: Sort models by (Provider,ID) in fetchModelsWithRaw + verify resolveCollisions","description":"---
references:

  fix_plan: bestiary-lyfs

## Scope
In cmd/bestiary-gen/main.go fetchModelsWithRaw, after the assembly loop (after line ~665,
before return), add:
  sort.Slice(models, func(i, j int) bool {
    if models[i].Provider != models[j].Provider { return models[i].Provider < models[j].Provider }
    return models[i].ID < models[j].ID
  })
This stabilizes every downstream consumer (generateSource, resolveCollisions, collectFamilies,
countUniqueProviders). (Provider, ID) must be a TOTAL order; sort.Slice is not stable so ties
would reintroduce non-determinism. Confirm (Provider, ID) uniqueness; add ID-only tiebreak only
if a tie is observed (it should not be, per (ID,Provider) composite key).
Verify resolveCollisions is deterministic given sorted input — it sorts positions within each
collision group and writes disjoint result slots, so map-iteration of nameToPositions does not
affect output. Expected: NO change to resolveCollisions.
